zones.name: North Lake Tahoe
zones.description: The quieter, outdoor-recreation-focused side of Lake Tahoe encompassing Truckee, Tahoe City, Kings Beach, and Incline Village. Features extensive trail networks, ski resorts (Palisades Tahoe, Northstar), and access to the Tahoe Rim Trail.

places.name: Big Chief Crag — Bouldering Base
places.address: Alpine Meadows Rd, Olympic Valley, CA 96146
places.parking_notes: Roadside pullout on Alpine Meadows Rd just off Hwy 89, ~0.3 mi from junction. Free, no permit. Tahoe National Forest land.

activities.notes: Granite crag at the base of Alpine Meadows Road with established bouldering problems on the lower cliffs. Part of the Tahoe National Forest. Mix of V0–V6 problems on solid Sierra granite. Developed by local Tahoe climbers; best accessed via short scramble from roadside pullout. Often quieter than Donner Summit on weekends. Year-round access when snow-free (May–Oct typical). No permit required.

places.name: Burton Creek State Park
places.address: Burton Creek State Park, northeast side of Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: Limited roadside parking at trailhead entry. No water, restrooms, or services on site. Open sunrise to sunset. No motorized vehicles.

activities.notes: Gentle fire road loops on northeast side of Tahoe City. Wildlife and bird watching. No traffic. Dogs allowed on fire roads (seasonal ski trail restrictions apply in winter).
Undeveloped fire road network near Tahoe City. Popular for trail running on packed dirt/gravel surfaces. Cross-country ski trails double as running routes in summer.
6 miles of unpaved fire roads through coniferous forest on south-facing slopes of the north shore Tahoe Basin. South-facing slopes, seasonal wildflowers in spring, fall cottonwood color.

places.name: Carnelian Bay Pier Walk
places.address: Carnelian Bay, CA 96140
places.parking_notes: Small public lot at Gar Woods pier; additional street parking on North Lake Blvd

activities.notes: Short lakeside stroll to historic Carnelian Bay pier. Fishing, sunset viewing. Nearby playground at Patton Landing Beach. Can combine with beach access.

places.name: Cascade Falls Trail
places.address: Cascade Rd, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: USFS lot on Cascade Rd; $5 fee; outhouses available

activities.notes: 1.5-mile round trip to Cascade Falls and Lake. Family-friendly with granite stream features. Waterfall best in late spring/early summer. Cool off in granite pools.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–June from snowmelt; can be dry by August

places.name: Castle Peak Trailhead – Donner Summit
places.address: Castle Peak Trailhead, off Donner Pass Rd / Castle Peak Rd, I-80 exit 176, Soda Springs, CA 95728
places.parking_notes: Free USFS parking lot at trailhead off I-80 exit 176 (Castle Valley / Soda Springs). Toilet available. No fee. Can fill on weekends.

activities.notes: 7-mile round-trip to Castle Peak summit (9,103 ft). ~1,850 ft total gain. Passes Castle Valley, sub-peak, and exposed rocky summit with 360-degree views of Donner Summit area and Sierra crest.

places.name: Donner Lake Rim Trail – Donner Memorial State Park Trailhead
places.address: Donner Memorial State Park, 12593 Donner Pass Rd, Truckee, CA 96161
places.parking_notes: Donner Memorial State Park day-use parking. Fee charged ($10/vehicle). Access to Donner Lake Rim Trail from east end of Donner Lake.

activities.notes: Out-and-back day hike on Donner Lake Rim Trail (~6 miles). Climb above Donner Lake for panoramic views of the lake and surrounding Sierra peaks. Trails range from moderate to strenuous.
Donner Lake Rim Trail full loop (~18.6 miles). Circumnavigates Donner Lake with views of the Sierra. Mix of singletrack and fire road. ~3,800 ft gain full loop. Popular trail run and race course.

places.name: Donner Peak Trailhead – Donner Summit
places.address: Donner Peak / Pacific Crest Trail Trailhead, Donner Pass Rd, Donner Pass, CA 96161
places.parking_notes: Small pullout parking at Donner Pass on old Hwy 40 / Donner Pass Rd. Free. Limited spaces. PCT northbound access from pass.

activities.notes: 4-mile round-trip to Donner Peak summit (8,019 ft) via PCT. ~1,250 ft gain. Excellent views of Donner Lake and Truckee basin. Rocky exposed terrain near summit. Short but steep final push.
4-mile round-trip to Donner Peak summit (8,019 ft) via PCT. ~1,250 ft gain. Excellent views of Donner Lake and Truckee basin. Rocky exposed terrain near summit. Short but steep final push.
activities.conditions_note: Start early Jul–Aug; afternoon thunderstorms common above treeline

places.name: Donner Summit Boulders
places.address: Donner Summit, Old Hwy 40, Truckee, CA 96161
places.parking_notes: Dirt pullouts along Old Hwy 40; free; fills weekends with climbers

activities.notes: Granite bouldering at 7,200 ft. Classics: 'Girls Gone Wild' (V1), 'Pimpsqueak' (V5). Best conditions July-Sept. Bring pads. Multiple areas along 1-mile stretch.

places.name: Eagle Lake Trail (Emerald Bay)
places.address: Eagle Lake Trailhead, Hwy 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: CA State Parks lot—fills by 9am summer; $10/vehicle; overflow at Vikingsholm lot

activities.notes: 2-mile round trip to alpine Eagle Lake above Emerald Bay. Steep switchbacks through granite. Lake swimming, granite slabs for lunch. Connects to Desolation Wilderness.
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

places.name: Eagle Rock Trail – Homewood Area
places.address: Eagle Rock Trailhead, Hwy 89 near Homewood, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: Small roadside pullout off Hwy 89 near Homewood. No fee. Very limited parking, arrive early.

activities.notes: Short 2-mile round-trip hike to Eagle Rock summit. ~400 ft gain. Outstanding panoramic views of Lake Tahoe and north shore. One of the best view hikes for effort in the area. Good for older children.

places.name: Ed Z'berg Sugar Pine Point State Park – Shoreline
places.address: Highway 89, 10 miles south of Tahoe City, Tahoma, CA 96142
places.parking_notes: Lakeside Day Use Area parking lot. $10/vehicle day use fee. Year-round self-pay when kiosk unstaffed. Visitors must park and walk to beach. Max trailer 26 ft.

activities.notes: Flat shoreline path with swimming beach, pier, and nature center with bird and wildlife exhibits. Hellman-Ehrman Mansion tour available. Excellent for children.
Nearly 2 miles of Lake Tahoe frontage along the west shore. Flat lakeside walk past Hellman-Ehrman Mansion (Pine Lodge), pier, and nature center. Over 20km of marked trails in park.

places.name: Five Lakes Trail – Granite Chief Wilderness Trailhead
places.address: Five Lakes Trailhead, Alpine Meadows Rd, near Palisades Tahoe, Olympic Valley, CA 96146
places.parking_notes: Small dirt parking area at end of Alpine Meadows Rd. Limited spaces (arrives full by 8–9am on weekends). No fee. No facilities.

activities.notes: 5-mile round-trip into Granite Chief Wilderness. Gains ~1,400 ft to a cluster of 5 alpine lakes at ~7,400 ft. Well-maintained trail with switchbacks through pine forest and granite. Swim in summer. Popular but rewarding.

places.name: Kings Beach Pier Walk
places.address: Kings Beach State Recreation Area, Kings Beach, CA 96143
places.parking_notes: CA State Parks fee $10/vehicle; multiple lots along lakefront; early arrival summer weekends

activities.notes: Flat paved lakefront stroll along Kings Beach with pier access and swimming beach. Playground and picnic tables nearby. Lifeguards in summer. One of the most accessible family beach walks on the north shore.
Paved lakefront promenade with pier access. Swimming beach with lifeguards in summer. Picnic areas, playground nearby. Excellent for strollers.

places.name: Kings Beach State Recreation Area
places.address: Kings Beach SRA, near Bear Street, Kings Beach, CA 96143
places.parking_notes: Year-round pay lots $10/day or $3/hour, 6am–9pm. Credit card only. RVs use Hwy 28/Raccoon lot. No beach parking for trailers.

activities.notes: 979 feet of sandy beach frontage on the north shore. Flat lakeside stroll along sandy beach. Playground, basketball half-court, 18 picnic tables under Jeffrey pines. Dogs on leash on sidewalks/picnic areas (not beach).

places.name: Lake Forest Beach Walk
places.address: Lake Forest Rd, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: Tahoe Conservancy lot; free but limited (20 spaces); street parking backup

activities.notes: Short trail through pine forest to secluded beach. Sandy coves for wading, rock skipping. Less crowded than Sand Harbor. Good for kids 3+.

places.name: Mount Judah Loop Peak
places.address: Donner Pass Rd, Soda Springs, CA 95728
places.parking_notes: Pacific Crest Trailhead lot at Donner Pass; free

activities.notes: 5-mile loop combining PCT and Pacific Crest alternate. Summit 8,243 ft. Old volcanic terrain, great views of Donner Summit area. Trail well-marked. Some loose rock on final approach but non-technical. Excellent intro to Sierra scrambling.
activities.conditions_note: Start early Jul–Aug; afternoon thunderstorms common above treeline

places.name: Mount Tallac Trail
places.address: Mount Tallac Trailhead, Fallen Leaf Lake Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: USFS dirt lot; fills by 8am weekends; no fee

activities.notes: 9.5-mile round trip to 9,735 ft summit. Best panoramic views in Tahoe region. 360-degree views of Lake Tahoe, Desolation Wilderness, Sierra Crest. Strenuous—start early, bring water.

places.name: Northstar California Trail Network
places.address: Northstar California Resort, 5001 Northstar Dr, Truckee, CA 96161
places.parking_notes: Village parking $5-15; free after 4pm; summer operations only

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-25K GROOMED] Resort-maintained trail network for summer use. 20+ miles of marked trails. 5K time-trial loop, 10K perimeter, 21K full network. Mix of singletrack and fire road. Maps at village. Water stops at resort base. Good for tempo runs. Chairlift access for downhill-only options.

places.name: Palisades Tahoe 5K/10K Trails
places.address: Palisades Tahoe, Olympic Valley, CA 96146
places.parking_notes: Resort parking $10-25/day; free shuttle from Village at Palisades Tahoe

activities.notes: Alpine running at 6,200+ ft. Summer trail race series (5K/10K). Mix of singletrack and fire road. Excellent training at altitude. Chairlift access for downhill options.
activities.conditions_note: Annual event — verify current dates and registration before planning your trip

places.name: Sand Harbor Beach Shoreline Walk
places.address: 2005 Highway 28, Incline Village, NV 89450
places.parking_notes: Pay parking lot on-site, $10 NV / $15 non-NV vehicles. Reservations required May 15–Sep 30 (8am–10:30am). No Hwy 28 roadside parking.

activities.notes: Sand Point Nature Trail (0.33 mi) is fully handicap-accessible with interpretive signs and lake views. Perfect for all ages and abilities.
Sand Harbor to Memorial Point Trail (0.5 mi one-way) plus Sand Point Nature Trail (0.33 mi). Flat, paved/packed-gravel shoreline. Crystal-clear water views. ADA-accessible sections.

places.name: Sand Harbor Beach Walk
places.address: Sand Harbor, Lake Tahoe-Nevada State Park, Incline Village, NV 89451
places.parking_notes: NV State Park fee area $10/vehicle; fills by 10am in summer; ADA accessible

activities.notes: Crystal-clear water walk on boardwalk and sand. East Shore's most scenic beach. Early morning arrival essential summer weekends. Wheelchair accessible boardwalk.

places.name: Sawtooth Ridge Trail Complex - PCT Access
places.address: Sawtooth Ridge Trailhead, Tahoe National Forest, CA 96148
places.parking_notes: USFS dirt lot; free; high clearance recommended; snow-free June-Oct

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 10K-42K+ TECHNICAL] PCT access point for serious trail runners. Options: 10K loop on old logging roads, 21K lollipop on PCT to Watson Lake, 42K+ point-to-point to Donner Summit. Technical singletrack, significant elevation. Training ground for ultra runners. Views of Lake Tahoe. Bring water—limited sources.

places.name: Spooner Lake Loop
places.address: Spooner Lake, Lake Tahoe Nevada State Park, Carson City, NV 89702
places.parking_notes: NV State Park fee area; large paved lot with vault toilets

activities.notes: 2-mile flat loop around Spooner Lake at 7,000 ft. Aspen groves, wildflowers July-Aug. Good birding. Mountain bike share trail—stay alert. Popular with families.

places.name: Tahoe City Boulders
places.address: Granite Chief Wilderness boundary near Ward Creek, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: Limited street parking on Ward Creek Rd; 4WD recommended for some areas

activities.notes: Glacial erratics and small crags. Grades V0-V7. Best spring/fall. Combine with hike to avoid crowds at Donner. Need local beta—less documented than Donner.

places.name: Tahoe City Commons Beach Trail Run Hub
places.address: Commons Beach, 400 North Lake Blvd, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: Free public lot; fills weekends; early arrival recommended

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-21K FLEXIBLE] Central trail running hub. Connects to: Truckee River Legacy Trail (8K out-and-back), Tahoe City to Squaw Valley bike path (16K one way), Burton Creek trails (5K-15K loop options). Paved and dirt mix. Flat to rolling. Multiple bailout points. Popular morning running destination.

places.name: Tahoe City Granite Slabs – General Creek Area
places.address: Ed Z'berg Sugar Pine Point State Park – General Creek Campground, Hwy 89, Tahoma, CA 96142
places.parking_notes: Park at Sugar Pine Point State Park General Creek Campground / day use area. $10/vehicle day use. Walk toward lake-adjacent granite outcroppings.

activities.notes: Granite outcroppings and slabs in the Sugar Pine Point / General Creek drainage area. Lower-angle granite features with juggy holds. Good for beginners and intermediates. Part of the broader west shore Tahoe granite band. Less developed than Donner Summit but scenic lakeside setting.

places.name: Tahoe City Marathon Loop
places.address: Commons Beach, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: Free public lot at Commons Beach; early arrival race days

activities.notes: Annual Tahoe City Marathon course (October). Mostly paved along lakefront and bike paths. Gentle rolling hills. Self-guided route any time—follow bike path network clockwise around lake for shorter options.
activities.conditions_note: Annual event — verify current dates and registration before planning your trip

places.name: Tahoe East Shore Trail (Sand Harbor Segment)
places.address: Tunnel Creek Staging Area, 1102 Tahoe Blvd, Incline Village, NV 89451
places.parking_notes: Park at Tunnel Creek Staging Area (1102 Tahoe Blvd). East Shore Express shuttle operates June–Aug. Day parking also at Sand Harbor lot.

activities.notes: Easy flat section of the paved East Shore Trail near Incline Village — Nevada's most scenic lakeside path. Crystal-clear water views across to the California shoreline. Wide, stroller-friendly path. Walk as far as you like and turn around; no destination required.
Out-and-back run on 3-mile paved East Shore Trail from Incline Village to Sand Harbor. Gentle grade, stunning lake views, minimal traffic.
3-mile paved multi-use bikeway from Incline Village south to Sand Harbor along Nevada's east shore. Flat, scenic lakeside path. Called 'America's Most Beautiful Bikeway'.

places.name: Tahoe Rim Trail - Brockway Summit Loop
places.address: Brockway Summit Trailhead, NV Hwy 431, Tahoe Vista, NV 96148
places.parking_notes: USFS dirt lot on Hwy 431; no facilities; winter closure Nov-May

activities.notes: 5-mile loop combining TRT with old fire roads. Singletrack with lake views, wildflower meadows. 8,300 ft elevation—acclimate before hard effort. Popular with trail runners.

places.name: Tahoe Rim Trail – Barker Pass Trailhead
places.address: Barker Pass Rd, off Hwy 89, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: Barker Pass Road (Blackwood Canyon Rd) off Hwy 89. Road open spring–fall; closed in winter depending on snowpack. Free parking at pass.

activities.notes: Barker Pass to Tahoe City (64 Acres): 16.4 miles one-way. Abundant wildlife, creeks, wildflowers, Page Meadow. Mix of forested ridgeline and open meadow running. Popular TRT segment for point-to-point runs with shuttle.

places.name: Tahoe Rim Trail – Brockway Summit Trailhead
places.address: Brockway Summit, Hwy 267, Kings Beach, CA 96143
places.parking_notes: Small pullout parking area at Brockway Summit on Hwy 267. No fee. Limited spaces. Access both north and south TRT segments.

activities.notes: Brockway Summit to Mt. Rose Summit / Tahoe Meadows: 20.2 miles one-way. Highest point on TRT is Relay Peak at 10,338 ft. Tremendous panoramic views. Requires shuttle or point-to-point logistics.

places.name: Tahoe Rim Trail – Tahoe City to Brockway Summit Trailhead (64 Acres)
places.address: 64 Acres TRT Trailhead, off Fairway Dr, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: 64 Acres trailhead parking lot. Plowed in winter months. Access via Fairway Dr near Tahoe City. No fee. Fiberboard Freeway gate closes seasonally.

activities.notes: Day hike out-and-back on TRT from 64 Acres toward Watson Lake (~5 miles one-way, 610m gain). Lava formations, cinder cones, forest, lake views. Watson Lake a scenic turnaround point.

Tahoe City (64 Acres) to Brockway Summit segment: 20.5 miles one-way. Rugged lava cliffs, cinder cones, Watson Lake (treat water), Truckee River Canyon views. One of the most scenic TRT segments. Suitable for experienced trail runners.

places.name: Tahoe Rim Trail – Twin Peaks Day Hike (Ward Creek Area)
places.address: Ward Creek Park Property, Tahoma, CA 96142 (Ward Creek Blvd access)
places.parking_notes: Limited parking at Ward Creek / Ward Creek Blvd trailhead. No fee. No water or restrooms. Open sunrise to sunset.

activities.notes: 9-mile round-trip day hike toward Twin Peaks (8,878 ft) via TRT from Ward Creek area. Dense forest transitions to sub-alpine terrain. ~3,000 ft gain. Solitary, undeveloped trails through Ward Creek's upland forest and meadowland.

places.name: Tahoe State Recreation Area – Tahoe City Commons Beach
places.address: Highway 28, 0.25 miles east of Tahoe City, Tahoe City, CA 96145
places.parking_notes: Small campground parking area. Limited day-use parking. Bike path connects campground to Tahoe City downtown.

activities.notes: Flat lakeside walking along Tahoe City's Commons Beach and adjacent bike path to downtown. Beach access, lake views, picnic areas. Bike path leads into downtown Tahoe City. Best combined with Truckee River Legacy Trail.

places.name: Truckee Legacy Trail – Town Section
places.parking_notes: Trailhead at Riverview Sports Park / Truckee Bike Park, 10700 Brockway Rd, Truckee. Free parking. Also accessible from downtown Truckee commercial area.

activities.notes: Paved multi-use path winding through downtown Truckee and along the Truckee River corridor. Connects Riverview Sports Park, the Truckee Bike Park pump track, downtown shops, and surrounding neighborhoods. Flat to gently rolling. Stroller- and dog-friendly. Restrooms at Riverview Sports Park.

Extended paved trail network through the town of Truckee, branching from the Riverview Sports Park hub toward Tahoe Donner, the airport area, and east-side neighborhoods. Connects to the Truckee River Legacy Trail (Tahoe City direction) and the Northstar path. Good for a longer outing or exploring the town on foot.

places.name: Truckee River Legacy Trail
places.address: Tahoe City, CA 96145 (trailhead at Commons Beach)
places.parking_notes: Street parking in Tahoe City; free public lot at Commons Beach; can fill by 11am weekends

activities.notes: Paved 4-mile (6.4 km) multi-use trail along Truckee River from Tahoe City to Squaw Valley. Gentle grade. River beaches for wading. Interpretive signs. Excellent for bikes, strollers, walking.

Paved 4-mile multi-use path along the Truckee River from Tahoe City northwest to the Squaw Valley Road junction near Palisades Tahoe. Parallels Hwy 89 through river canyon. Mostly flat; gentle undulations. Multiple river-access pullouts and bench spots. Out-and-back or one-way with car shuttle.

places.name: Truckee–Northstar Multi-Use Path
places.parking_notes: Western trailhead at Riverview Sports Park / Truckee Bike Park (10700 Brockway Rd, Truckee). Eastern end near Northstar California Village — park at the Village at Northstar day lots.

activities.notes: Paved multi-use path from the Truckee Bike Park area southeast through Martis Valley toward Northstar California. Open valley views toward the Sierra crest. First 3 miles are the gentlest and most accessible for families. Popular with cyclists — stay to the right. The Truckee Bike Park pump track is a bonus stop for kids at the trailhead.

Full 7-mile paved route from downtown Truckee to the Northstar resort base via the Hwy 267 corridor. Rolling terrain through open Martis Valley with mountain views. Makes for a solid out-and-back or one-way with a car shuttle. Biking is the dominant use — most walkers do the first few miles.

places.name: West Shore Bike Path – Tahoe City to Homewood
places.parking_notes: North end: Commons Beach / 64 Acres park in Tahoe City (64 N Lake Blvd). South end: roadside pullouts near Homewood/Tahoma. Multiple mid-route access points at Sunnyside (1850 W Lake Blvd) and Tahoe Pines.

activities.notes: Flat paved multi-use path from Tahoe City heading south along the west shore. Spectacular lake views the entire way. Runs safely away from Hwy 89 for most of the route. Multiple beach access points for a swim break. Turn around at Sunnyside (2.5 mi) for an easy round trip. Stroller- and dog-friendly.

Paved lakeside path from Tahoe City to Homewood and beyond toward Tahoma, about 6.5 miles one-way. West shore of Lake Tahoe with views across to Mount Rose and the Nevada range. Shade from pines and aspens. Beach pullouts at Sunnyside, Obexer's, and Chamber Beach near Homewood. Return same way or arrange a shuttle.

places.name: Western States 100 Start – Palisades Tahoe / Olympic Valley
places.address: Palisades Tahoe Base Village, 1960 Squaw Valley Rd, Olympic Valley, CA 96146
places.parking_notes: Large Palisades Tahoe parking lots at base village. Free general parking. Race start is at west end of Olympic Valley at 5am on race day (last full weekend of June).

activities.notes: Western States 100 Mile Endurance Run. Starts at 6,200 ft elevation, climbs 2,550 ft to Emigrant Pass (8,750 ft) in first 4.5 miles. Total ~18,090 ft gain / ~22,970 ft descent to Auburn, CA. One of the world's most prestigious ultramarathons. Annual event last full weekend of June.
activities.conditions_note: Annual event — verify current dates and registration before planning your trip

zones.name: South Lake Tahoe
zones.description: The larger, entertainment-and-recreation resort side of Lake Tahoe, featuring Heavenly ski resort, Emerald Bay State Park, Desolation Wilderness, and numerous beaches. Distinct from North Tahoe in visitor demographics and amenity profile.

places.name: Angora Ridge Boulder Field (near Fallen Leaf Lake)
places.address: Angora Ridge Rd off Fallen Leaf Lake Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (El Dorado National Forest / LTBMU)
places.parking_notes: Informal pullouts along Angora Ridge Rd. Free, no fee. High-clearance vehicle recommended for upper road. ~5–8 cars at various pullouts. Angora Lookout (fire lookout) at road end provides additional parking.

activities.notes: Granite bouldering on Angora Ridge: scattered granite boulders with V0–V6 problems on high-quality Sierra Nevada granite at ~7,200 ft. Informal/unestablished area. Short approach from Angora Ridge Rd. Beautiful forest setting, views of Fallen Leaf Lake. Best May–Oct.

places.name: Bayview Trailhead (Granite Lake / Desolation Wilderness)
places.address: Bayview Campground, Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (10 mi north of SLT, near Emerald Bay)
places.parking_notes: Bayview Campground day-use parking off Hwy 89. Small lot ~15 cars. USFS. Desolation Wilderness day permit required (self-issue). Campground adjacent (recreation.gov reservations).

activities.notes: Granite Lake via Bayview Trail: 2.0 mi RT, 900 ft gain in first mile. Short but steep to beautiful alpine Granite Lake in Desolation Wilderness. Wildflowers June–Aug. Permit required.
Bayview Trail to Velma Lakes (7.5 mi RT): Extended Desolation Wilderness route from Bayview through Granite Lake to Velma Lakes chain. Stunning granite alpine terrain. Permit required.

places.name: Big Meadow Trailhead (Tahoe Rim Trail)
places.address: Luther Pass Rd (Hwy 89), Meyers, CA 96150 (3 mi south of SLT on Hwy 89 near Luther Pass)
places.parking_notes: Free USFS parking area off Hwy 89 at Big Meadow. ~25 spaces. No fee. TRT trailhead signage. No permit required for this TRT section. Restrooms at trailhead.

activities.notes: Big Meadow to Big Meadow Creek: short family walk on TRT through open meadow with stream crossing. 2 mi RT, mostly flat. Wildflowers in summer. Easy access off Hwy 89.
Big Meadow Loop (TRT): ~6 mi via TRT through open meadows and conifer forest with wildflower displays. Views of Freel Peak. Moderate terrain on Tahoe Rim Trail. No permit required.
TRT Big Meadow to Round Lake segment run: ~9 mi RT on Tahoe Rim Trail through meadows, aspen groves, and Meiss Meadows. High-altitude trail run at 7,200–8,400 ft. Non-technical, flowy singletrack.

places.name: Cascade Falls
places.address: Bayview Campground Trailhead, Hwy 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: Bayview Campground parking area on east side of Hwy 89. Day use parking on shoulder. No fee for day use at trailhead. Fills quickly on summer weekends.

activities.notes: Popular family destination for waterfall viewing. Short enough for older kids (5+). Rocky terrain requires sturdy shoes. Peak flow late spring. Stay back from waterfall edges — rocks are slippery. No restrooms at trailhead.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–June from snowmelt; can be dry by August

activities.notes: El Dorado National Forest trail to Cascade Falls — 100+ ft waterfall on Cascade Creek. 1.6 km each way. Rocky terrain, some boulder scrambling near falls. Best flow spring through early July. Spectacular views of Cascade Lake below.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–June from snowmelt; can be dry by August

places.name: Cascade Falls Trailhead
places.address: Cascade Road off Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (near Cascade Lake, south of Emerald Bay)
places.parking_notes: Small roadside parking area on Cascade Rd near Hwy 89. Free, no fee. Limited spaces (~10-15 cars). No facilities. Overflow park along Hwy 89 shoulder.

activities.notes: Cascade Falls is a popular family destination – spectacular waterfall 2.0 mi RT. Rocky sections near the end require care with small children. Best May–July when flow is strong.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–June from snowmelt; can be dry by August

activities.notes: Cascade Falls Trail: 1.0 mi one-way to Cascade Falls (over 200 ft tall waterfall). Best in late spring/early summer. Moderate rocky trail with great payoff. Some scrambling near falls. Dog-friendly on leash.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–June from snowmelt; can be dry by August

places.name: D.L. Bliss State Park – Rubicon Trail Trailhead
places.address: D.L. Bliss State Park, Highway 89, Tahoma, CA 96142 (17 mi north of South Lake Tahoe on Hwy 89)
places.parking_notes: State Park day-use lot. $10–$12/day. NOTE: D.L. Bliss State Park closed to vehicles and pedestrians in 2025 for construction. Check parks.ca.gov for current status. Rubicon Trail currently accessed from Emerald Bay end only.

activities.notes: Rubicon Trail (full traverse, D.L. Bliss to Vikingsholm): ~5.5 mi one-way along Lake Tahoe shoreline. One of the most scenic lakeside trails in the Sierra Nevada. No dogs. Park currently closed 2025 – access from Emerald Bay end instead.
activities.conditions_note: State park entrance; day use fee applies

activities.notes: Rubicon Trail north section near D.L. Bliss beach area: shorter lake-level walk on one of California's most scenic shoreline trails. Crystal-clear water, granite slabs. No dogs.
activities.conditions_note: State park entrance; day use fee applies

places.name: D.L. Bliss State Park — Shoreline Walk
places.address: D.L. Bliss State Park, Hwy 89, Tahoma, CA 96142
places.parking_notes: D.L. Bliss State Park entrance off Hwy 89. Day use fee. Rubicon Trail parking near Calawee Cove Beach.

activities.notes: Family walk to Calawee Cove — one of Lake Tahoe's best accessible sandy beaches within D.L. Bliss SP. Short walk from parking. Restrooms at beach. Calmer water than open South Shore. Good for small kids.
activities.conditions_note: State park entrance; day use fee applies

activities.notes: California State Parks shoreline trail through D.L. Bliss SP along the western shore of Lake Tahoe. Crystal-clear water, granite boulders, and forested slopes. Connects to Vikingsholm/Emerald Bay as part of the Rubicon Trail. Light traffic compared to busier South Shore spots.
activities.conditions_note: State park entrance; day use fee applies

places.name: Eagle Falls Trailhead
places.address: Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (adjacent to Emerald Bay, 0.3 mi north of Vikingsholm parking)
places.parking_notes: USFS fee area parking lot off Hwy 89. Fee applies. Shuttle pickup point for Emerald Bay Shuttle (through Oct 15). Dogs allowed (USFS land). Very limited spaces – arrive before 8 am on weekends.

activities.notes: Eagle Falls Lower Loop: short family-friendly hike from trailhead to Lower Eagle Falls and back. Dogs allowed. Impressive waterfall view; some rocky terrain near falls.
Eagle Lake via Eagle Falls Trail: 3.0 mi round trip from trailhead climbing through granite to alpine Eagle Lake (elev. 7,421 ft). Desolation Wilderness – day permit required. Rocky, strenuous.
Lower Eagle Falls viewpoint: short walk behind the visitor area to view the lower cascade of Eagle Falls. 0.25 mi each way. Paved path to viewpoint.

places.name: Echo Lakes Trailhead (Lower Echo Lake)
places.address: Johnson Pass Rd off Highway 50, Echo Summit, CA 95762 (Echo Lakes Rd from Hwy 50, 1 mi north of Echo Summit)
places.parking_notes: USFS fee parking lot at Lower Echo Lake. $10/day. Water taxi available across Echo Lakes in summer (paid service). Desolation Wilderness permit required for Wilderness entry. Tahoe Rim Trail trailhead also here.

activities.notes: Echo Lakes shoreline walk to Upper Echo Lake: flat, scenic, kid-friendly. Granite boulders to scramble on. Water taxi option adds excitement for kids. Fee parking.
Echo Lakes shoreline walk: flat trail around Lower and Upper Echo Lake. 2.5 mi RT to Upper Echo Lake dam. Stunning granite scenery, no significant elevation. Optional water taxi shortcut.
Echo Lakes to Lake of the Woods via PCT/Desolation Wilderness: 9 mi RT. Classic Desolation Wilderness dayhike through granite basins. Permit required. Water taxi can reduce distance.
Echo Lakes flat run along shoreline: excellent high-altitude (7,400 ft) trail run on relatively flat, packed-dirt trail. Out-and-back to Upper Echo Lake. Stunning alpine scenery.

places.name: Echo Lakes to Desolation Wilderness
places.address: Echo Lakes Chalet, Johnson Pass Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: Private lot at Echo Lakes Chalet; fee for day use; boat taxi available

activities.notes: 7.5-mile round trip to Upper Echo Lake and beyond. Boardwalk trail along lakes. Connects to PCT/JMT. Alpine scenery, granite peaks. Boat taxi cuts 2.5 miles each way.

places.name: Echo Summit Roadside Bouldering
places.address: Echo Summit, Hwy 50, El Dorado County, CA 95726
places.parking_notes: Pullouts along Hwy 50 at Echo Summit (7,382 ft). Multiple small areas. Free. Restrooms at Echo Summit Sno-Park in winter.

activities.notes: Granite boulders immediately accessible from Hwy 50 pullouts at Echo Summit (7,382 ft). El Dorado National Forest. Grades V0–V5. Great high-elevation bouldering stopover on the way from South Lake Tahoe to Desolation Wilderness. Year-round access when road is clear of snow.

places.name: El Dorado Beach & Lakeview Commons Walk
places.parking_notes: Parking at El Dorado Beach (1000 Rufus Allen Blvd, South Lake Tahoe) or Lakeview Commons (1000 Lakeview Ave, South Lake Tahoe). Free city parking lots.

activities.notes: Flat paved lakefront walk through South Lake Tahoe's main city beach area. Connects El Dorado Beach, Lakeview Commons grassy park, and the adjoining bike path. Playground, volleyball courts, grills, and pier. Accessible to all ages and abilities. Lakeview Commons has a nice lawn for picnicking with direct lake views.

Extended paved lakefront walk connecting El Dorado Beach, Lakeview Commons, and the South Lake Tahoe paved bike path east toward the Stateline area. Flat, well-surfaced, open lake views throughout. One of the few paved lakefront paths directly accessible from the city center.

places.name: Emerald Bay Loop Run
places.address: D.L. Bliss State Park, Hwy 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: CA State Parks lot $10; fills by 9am; seasonal closure mid-Oct to Memorial Day

activities.notes: 5.3-mile loop combining Rubicon Trail and road sections. Scenic singletrack above Emerald Bay. Technical in sections. Best fall colors September-October.
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

places.name: Emerald Bay Overlook / Inspiration Point
places.address: Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (Vikingsholm parking lot area, 12 mi north of SLT)
places.parking_notes: Shared with Vikingsholm Trail parking. $10/day or $3/hr. Pull-off viewpoint also available 0.2 mi north on Hwy 89 (very limited, 3–4 cars). The overlook itself is 0.1 mi flat walk from Vikingsholm lot.

activities.notes: Best family photo stop on Lake Tahoe – Emerald Bay overlook with Vikingsholm Castle and Fannette Island below. Nearly flat walk from parking. Combine with Vikingsholm Castle hike for longer excursion.
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

activities.notes: Emerald Bay Overlook: 0.1 mi flat walk from Vikingsholm parking to the iconic Emerald Bay viewpoint. One of the most photographed spots in California. Accessible to all fitness levels. Short but spectacular.
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

places.name: Emerald Bay State Park – Vikingsholm Trailhead Parking
places.address: Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (12 mi north of South Lake Tahoe, 22 mi south of Tahoe City)
places.parking_notes: Vikingsholm parking lot off Hwy 89. $10/day or $3/hr. Max vehicle length 18 ft. Fills by mid-morning in summer. Emerald Bay Shuttle available at Eagle Falls Trailhead through Oct 15 as overflow alternative.

activities.notes: Walk down to Vikingsholm Castle – a Scandinavian replica on Emerald Bay. Kids enjoy the castle architecture and lake views. Steep return uphill; bring water. No dogs allowed on this trail.
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

activities.notes: Rubicon Trail from Vikingsholm south toward Eagle Point: scenic foot trail wrapping around Emerald Bay. No dogs. DL Bliss State Park closed 2025 (vehicle/pedestrian); access from Emerald Bay side only. Approximately 2.5 mi one-way to Eagle Point.
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

activities.notes: Vikingsholm Trail: steep 1-mi descent to Vikingsholm Castle on Emerald Bay shore, return 400 ft gain. Paved path. No rides out – plan for uphill return. Emerald Bay overlook at trailhead is a shorter walk option (0.1 mi, flat).
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

places.name: Emerald Bay State Park — Shoreline Walk
places.address: Emerald Bay State Park, Emerald Bay Rd (Hwy 89), South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: Emerald Bay Overlook parking on Hwy 89 (day use fee). Shuttle from South Lake Tahoe recommended in summer — lot fills by 9 AM. Vikingsholm parking lot is lower and larger.

activities.notes: Family walk to Vikingsholm Castle on Emerald Bay — rated one of the finest examples of Scandinavian architecture in North America. Free to walk to; castle tours fee. Beach at bay bottom. Bring water and snacks; no services below overlook.
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

activities.notes: Walk from Vikingsholm parking area down to Emerald Bay beach and historic Vikingsholm Castle (tours available summer). 160 m descent/ascent. California State Parks-managed. Strenuous for small children on return. One of the most photographed spots in California.
activities.conditions_note: Hwy 89 may close in winter; parking fills by 9am on summer weekends

places.name: Fallen Leaf Lake Road Trailhead Area
places.address: 2165 Fallen Leaf Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: Multiple informal pullouts along Fallen Leaf Lake Rd. Glen Alpine Trailhead at road end has ~20 free spaces. Fallen Leaf Lake Campground (recreation.gov reservations) adjacent. No day-use fee for road walk.

activities.notes: Fallen Leaf Lake Road walk: flat paved road walk along the west shore of Fallen Leaf Lake. Stunning lake and mountain views. Turn around at campground area or continue further. Excellent for all ages.

Angora Lakes trail from Fallen Leaf Lake Rd: 1.7 mi one-way to lower Angora Lake, continuing to Upper Angora Lake. Steep uphill. Stunning granite cirque. Angora Lakes Resort at upper lake (seasonal refreshments).

Fallen Leaf Lake Road out-and-back run: 6-mi lollipop along paved road and dirt track around the lake, starting from Hwy 89 junction. Popular local run at ~6,400 ft elevation. Mostly flat with minor grades.
Fallen Leaf Lake shoreline walk: paved road and informal trails along the 2-mi-long lake. Popular with families and casual walkers. Views of Mt. Tallac reflected in lake.

places.name: Fallen Leaf Lake Trail Running Base
places.address: Fallen Leaf Lake Marina, Fallen Leaf Lake Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: Marina lot $5; street parking on Fallen Leaf Rd; fills weekends

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 8K-25K ALPINE] Glen Alpine Trail access for mountain runners. 8K to Glen Alpine Falls, 16K lollipop to Susie Lake, 25K+ to Gilmore Lake/Desolation Wilderness boundary. 6,300-8,500 ft elevation. Technical rocky sections. Cascade views, alpine lakes. Bear country awareness. Popular with ultra training groups.

places.name: Fallen Leaf Lake Walk
places.address: Fallen Leaf Lake, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: USFS day-use lot $5; street parking on Fallen Leaf Lake Rd; fills summer weekends

activities.notes: 3-mile loop along Fallen Leaf Lake shore. Quiet alternative to busy Lake Tahoe beaches. Granite boulders for scrambling. Less crowded than main lake. Non-motorized only.

places.name: Glen Alpine Trailhead (Desolation Wilderness)
places.address: Glen Alpine Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (off Fallen Leaf Lake Rd, El Dorado National Forest)
places.parking_notes: Free USFS parking at Glen Alpine Springs area. ~20 spaces. Self-issue Desolation Wilderness permit at trailhead. Fallen Leaf Lake Rd from Hwy 89; continue past Fallen Leaf Lake to road end.

activities.notes: Glen Alpine to Lake of the Woods / Gilmore Lake: 4–6 mi one-way into Desolation Wilderness. Multiple lake destinations. Wilderness permit required. Granite and pine terrain, alpine lakes. Elevation starts ~6,560 ft.
Glen Alpine Trail to Grass Lake: 3.5 mi RT, easier Desolation Wilderness entry. Excellent for a first wilderness hike. Permit required.

places.name: Heavenly Mountain – Tamarack Trail (Summer)
places.address: 3860 Saddle Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (Heavenly Village Gondola base)
places.parking_notes: Heavenly Village parking structures and lots in downtown South Lake Tahoe. $10–$30/day depending on lot. Gondola fee required to access upper mountain trails ($35–$45 adult). Free viewing area at base.

activities.notes: Heavenly Tamarack Trail (summer): gondola to observation deck at 9,123 ft, then hike along ridgeline trail with 360° views of Lake Tahoe and Nevada. Gondola fee required. Well-marked resort trails.
Heavenly Mountain observation deck walk: take gondola to top, short interpretive walk to observation deck at 9,123 ft. Views stretch to Nevada desert and Lake Tahoe simultaneously.

places.name: Heavenly Village Promenade
places.address: Heavenly Village, 1001 Heavenly Village Way, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: Paid lots in Heavenly Village; 2hr free at some shops with validation

activities.notes: Paved pedestrian village with shops, restaurants, fountains for kids. Gondola base. Not wilderness but good family walk combined with gondola ride for mountain views.

places.name: Kiva Beach (USFS Day-Use Area)
places.address: Kiva Beach Rd off Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (2 mi north of SLT city limits, near LTBMU Visitor Center)
places.parking_notes: USFS Kiva Beach day-use lot off Hwy 89. Fee area. Connects via shoreline path to Camp Richardson (1.5 mi west) and to South Lake Tahoe city beach (2 mi east). Restrooms and picnic area on site.

activities.notes: Kiva Beach family walk: flat beach and forest loop connecting Kiva Beach picnic area, shoreline, and forest path. Ideal for families with young children. Sandy beach access for wading.
South Shore shoreline run: Kiva Beach east toward El Dorado Beach and South Lake Tahoe beach access. ~3–6 mi depending on turnaround. Paved multi-use path and sandy trails. Flat and fast.
Kiva Beach to Pope Beach shoreline walk: ~1.5 mi one-way on flat sandy/forest path along Lake Tahoe south shore. Connects multiple USFS beach areas. Stunning lake views.

places.name: Lake Tahoe Marathon Events
places.address: Lakeview Commons, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: Free street parking near commons; shuttles to start lines for races

activities.notes: Annual Lake Tahoe Marathon (October) with 5K, half, full, 72-mile ultra options. Course around lake on bike paths and trails. Self-guided training routes available year-round.
activities.conditions_note: Annual October event — verify dates at laketahoemarathon.com

places.name: Lake Tahoe Marathon – Start/Finish (Cave Rock Area)
places.address: Cave Rock, Highway 50, Zephyr Cove, NV 89448 (south shore Lake Tahoe, annual event varies location)
places.parking_notes: Event parking at designated areas for race day. Free participant parking. Check race-specific instructions each year. South shore lake corridor.

activities.notes: Lake Tahoe Marathon (annual event, typically October): paved/trail course around south Lake Tahoe corridor at ~6,200 ft elevation. Also offers half-marathon (21.1 km) and 10-mi options. Altitude significantly increases difficulty.
activities.conditions_note: Annual October event — verify dates at laketahoemarathon.com

places.name: Lover's Leap Bouldering
places.address: Lover's Leap, Hwy 50, Twin Bridges, CA 95735
places.parking_notes: USFS lot on Hwy 50; free; 0.3 mile hike to cliff base

activities.notes: World-famous granite crag at 5,720 ft on South Fork American River near Strawberry, El Dorado NF. Primarily sport/trad climbing (5.6–5.12) but excellent bouldering on lower crags and approach slabs. V0–V8. Best season April–November. BLM/USFS access — no permit required. ~45 min from South Lake Tahoe.

places.name: Lover's Leap Climbing Area
places.address: Lover's Leap Rd off Highway 50, Strawberry, CA 95684 (El Dorado National Forest, ~7 mi east of Kyburz, ~25 mi from South Lake Tahoe)
places.parking_notes: Free USFS gravel parking area along Hwy 50 and Lover's Leap Rd near Strawberry. No fee. No reservations. Pit toilet on site. 30–40 spaces. Can fill on summer/fall weekends.

activities.notes: World-famous granite crag at 5,720 ft on South Fork American River near Strawberry, El Dorado NF. Primarily sport/trad climbing (5.6–5.12) but excellent bouldering on lower crags and approach slabs. V0–V8. Best season April–November. BLM/USFS access — no permit required. ~45 min from South Lake Tahoe.
Lover's Leap summit trail: hike above the wall to summit plateau at ~6,800 ft for expansive views of American River canyon. 3 mi RT. Steep in sections. Use climbers' trail from parking.

places.name: Maggies Peaks Trail
places.address: Bayview Trailhead, Hwy 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: USFS lot $5; fills by 8am; overflow at D.L. Bliss

activities.notes: 6-mile round trip to Maggies Peaks with Desolation Wilderness access. Panoramic views of Fallen Leaf Lake and Lake Tahoe. Granite slabs, wildflowers. Class 2 scramble to true summit.

places.name: Mount Rose Summit Walk-up
places.address: Mount Rose Summit Trailhead, NV-431, NV 89411
places.parking_notes: NV State Park lot at 8,911 ft; $10 day use; seasonal closure winter

activities.notes: 2.5-mile round trip to 10,779 ft—highest peak in Tahoe basin. Trail from summit parking. Gradual grade, wide switchbacks. Dramatic views of Lake Tahoe. Often snow until July. Good for building altitude tolerance. No scrambling required.
activities.conditions_note: Start early Jul–Aug; afternoon thunderstorms common above treeline

places.name: Mt. Tallac Trailhead
places.address: Mt. Tallac Rd off Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (3.5 mi north of South Lake Tahoe on Hwy 89, left onto Mt. Tallac Rd)
places.parking_notes: Free USFS parking lot at end of Mt. Tallac Rd. ~30 spaces. No fee for day use. Desolation Wilderness day-use permit required at trailhead self-issue station. Fills by 7 am on summer weekends.

activities.notes: Mt. Tallac Trail: ~10 mi RT, summit 9,735 ft. Classic South Shore hike via Tallac Lake and the Cross snowfield to summit overlooking Lake Tahoe. 3,000+ ft gain. Desolation Wilderness permit required. Strenuous, exposed summit section.
Mt. Tallac summit run – popular with trail runners. High-altitude exposure above 9,000 ft. Rocky technical terrain on upper section. Best July–September. Permit required.

places.name: Phantom Spires Bouldering — El Dorado NF
places.address: Phantom Spires Trailhead, Ice House Rd, El Dorado National Forest, CA 95726
places.parking_notes: Small parking pullout on Ice House Road, El Dorado National Forest. No fee. Rough road access — high clearance recommended.

activities.notes: El Dorado National Forest volcanic rock bouldering and top-rope climbing area near Sierra-at-Tahoe resort. Several distinct spires and boulder fields. Grades range V0–V8. Solitude compared to Lover's Leap. Best season May–October. No permit required.

places.name: Pie Shop Crag — Bouldering
places.address: Hwy 50 (US-50), Twin Bridges, CA 95735
places.parking_notes: Roadside pullout on south side of US-50 at Twin Bridges, El Dorado National Forest. Free, no fee. 0.2 mi walk to Pie Shop wall.

activities.notes: Pie Shop is a popular El Dorado National Forest granite crag at the base of the Lover's Leap formation on US-50, with accessible bouldering on the lower walls. Problems range V0–V5, on excellent featured Sierra granite. A shorter approach than the main Lover's Leap routes makes this a convenient session stop. Also a key transition wall for beginners moving from bouldering to sport climbing. Best season May–October. No permit required.

places.name: Pope Beach / Camp Richardson Shoreline
places.address: Pope Beach Rd off Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (adjacent to Camp Richardson Resort)
places.parking_notes: USFS Pope Beach day-use parking lot, fee area ($10–$15/day). Camp Richardson Resort parking also available with lodging/dining. Paved lot, restrooms, beach access. Very busy July–Aug.

activities.notes: Camp Richardson beach walk: flat sandy trail from Camp Richardson marina to Pope Beach. Ideal for families – flat, short, beautiful lake and mountain views. Beach swimming in summer.
South Shore Lake Tahoe shoreline run: Pope Beach to Camp Richardson to Kiva Beach and back. ~6 mi RT on flat sandy/packed trail along lake. Beautiful mountain backdrop. Popular morning run.
Pope Beach to Camp Richardson shoreline walk: flat sandy beach and forest path along Lake Tahoe's south shore. 1.5 mi one-way. Family-friendly, views of Emerald Bay and mountains across the lake.

places.name: Pope Beach Family Loop
places.address: Pope Beach, Pope Beach Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lot $10/vehicle; ADA spaces; restrooms available

activities.notes: 1.5-mile sandy beach walk with picnic areas. Gentle lake entry for kids. Buoys mark swimming area. Restrooms and concessions in summer. Less crowded than Nevada Beach.

places.name: Powerline Trail Network - Meyers Access
places.address: Meyers, CA 96150 (Powerline Trail access via Pioneer Trail)
places.parking_notes: Dirt lot at trail junction; free; no facilities; 4WD helpful

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-20K HILL TRAINING] Old powerline service roads converted to trails. Consistent 5-8% grades—perfect hill training. 5K repeats, 10K out-and-back, 20K point-to-point to Christmas Valley. Views of Freel Peak. Less crowded than lakeside trails. Good for winter training when high trails snowed in. Self-sufficient required.

places.name: Pyramid Creek Trailhead (Horsetail Falls / Desolation Wilderness)
places.address: Highway 50, Twin Bridges, CA 95735 (3 mi west of Echo Summit on Hwy 50)
places.parking_notes: USFS paid parking area on Hwy 50 at Twin Bridges. $5–$10/day. Very busy in summer; fills by 9 am on weekends. Desolation Wilderness day permit required. Restrooms on site.

activities.notes: Horsetail Falls Trail from Pyramid Creek: 3.5 mi RT, ~1,700 ft gain to the base of Horsetail Falls (500 ft waterfall in stages). Very steep rocky scramble to upper falls. Desolation Wilderness permit required above falls. Best June–early August.
Pyramid Creek to Lake of the Woods trail run: ~6 mi RT, technical rocky ascent. Desolation Wilderness permit required. High-altitude (7,000–8,400 ft). Only for experienced trail runners.

places.name: Ralston Peak Trail
places.address: Ralston Peak Trailhead, US-50 near Wrights Lake, CA 95720
places.parking_notes: Wrights Lake Rd then 4WD forest road; high clearance recommended

activities.notes: 7-mile round trip to 9,235 ft. Steep but well-defined trail through forest to granite summit. 360° views of Lake Tahoe and Desolation Wilderness. Last 200 yards are loose scree—take your time. Good fitness test peak.
activities.conditions_note: Start early Jul–Aug; afternoon thunderstorms common above treeline

places.name: Ralston Peak Trailhead
places.address: Highway 50, Echo Summit, CA 95762 (just west of Echo Summit, Hwy 50 pullout)
places.parking_notes: Small roadside pullout on Hwy 50 at Echo Summit area. ~10 vehicles. No fee. Desolation Wilderness day permit required (self-issue at trailhead). No facilities.

activities.notes: Ralston Peak Trail: ~8 mi RT to Ralston Peak summit (9,235 ft). Desolation Wilderness entry via Echo Summit trailhead. Sweeping views of Lake Tahoe and Desolation Wilderness. Rocky upper section. Best July–October.

places.name: Spooner Summit Trail Running Hub
places.address: Spooner Summit, Hwy 50, South Lake Tahoe, NV 89449
places.parking_notes: NV State Park lot; $10 day use; large paved area; restrooms

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-30K+ POINT-TO-POINT] FLume Trail north access point. World-famous lake-view running. 5K preview loop, 10K to Marlette Lake, 21K to Tunnel Creek, 30K+ point-to-point to Incline Village. High elevation 7,000-8,500 ft. Exposed—early start essential. Bucket-list Sierra running destination. Bring electrolytes.

places.name: Tahoe Rim Trail - Kingsbury Grade
places.address: Kingsbury Grade Trailhead, NV Hwy 207, Stateline, NV 89449
places.parking_notes: NV DOT lot; free; steep access road; no facilities

activities.notes: 7.5-mile out-and-back on TRT with 1,600 ft gain. High ridge running with lake views. Singletrack with some technical sections. Start early—exposed after 9am.

places.name: Taylor Creek Stream Trail
places.address: Taylor Creek Visitor Center, 870 Emerald Bay Rd, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: Free parking at Taylor Creek Visitor Center (USFS). Fills on summer weekends. Flush toilets on site.

activities.notes: NPS/USFS top family pick for South Lake Tahoe. Salmon viewing in October is an unmissable kid experience. Flat boardwalk trail, stroller accessible. Visitor Center has hands-on exhibits. Restrooms at trailhead.
activities.conditions_note: Kokanee salmon spawn typically mid-October — the main event

activities.notes: USFS-designated easy interpretive walk along Taylor Creek to Lake Tahoe. Famous for annual Kokanee salmon spawn (Oct). Boardwalk through meadow and willows. Wheelchair accessible sections. Stream profile chamber lets visitors view fish underwater.
activities.conditions_note: Kokanee salmon spawn typically mid-October — the main event

places.name: Taylor Creek Stream Trail Trailhead
places.address: Highway 89, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 (Taylor Creek Visitor Center, 3.5 mi north of SLT city limits)
places.parking_notes: Free day-use parking lot at Taylor Creek Visitor Center off Hwy 89. Fills early in fall during salmon run season (Oct).

activities.notes: Best family walk in the South Shore. Paved/compacted gravel, stroller-accessible much of the way. October kokanee salmon run is a highlight for kids. Stream Profile Chamber provides underwater fish viewing.
activities.conditions_note: Kokanee salmon spawn typically mid-October — the main event

activities.notes: Taylor Creek Stream Trail: flat loop along Taylor Creek through meadows and aspen groves to Lake Tahoe shoreline. Stream Profile Chamber underwater viewing window mid-route.
activities.conditions_note: Kokanee salmon spawn typically mid-October — the main event

places.name: Van Sickle Bi-State Park Loop
places.address: Van Sickle Bi-State Park, Stateline, NV 89449
places.parking_notes: Shared CA/NV park lot off Heavenly Village Way; $8/day; can fill weekends

activities.notes: 2.5-mile loop on old fire road and singletrack. Views of Lake Tahoe and Heavenly Valley. Connector to Tahoe Rim Trail. Good option near town with moderate effort.

places.name: Vikingsholm Trail
places.address: Vikingsholm, Emerald Bay, South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150
places.parking_notes: CA State Parks lot $10; fills by 9am; 0.7 mile walk down from parking

activities.notes: 1.4-mile round trip to Vikingsholm mansion (Swedish castle replica) on Emerald Bay shore. Historic home tours in summer. Steep paved path. Beautiful bay views.

zones.name: Tuolumne Meadows
zones.description: High-alpine subalpine meadow at 8,619 ft in Yosemite National Park, accessed via Tioga Road (Highway 120). Distinct from Yosemite Valley — more remote, less crowded, with access to PCT/JMT, Cathedral Lakes, and extensive high-country hiking. Summer-only access (road closes Nov–May).

places.name: Cathedral Lakes Loop Run
places.address: Cathedral Lakes Trailhead, Tioga Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: NPS paved lot off Tioga Road; fills by 8am; wilderness permit required for overnight

activities.notes: 8-mile loop to Lower and Upper Cathedral Lakes at 9,200 ft. Steep initial 2 miles (1,000 ft gain). Alpine lakes beneath Cathedral Peak. Technical rocky sections. Best July-October.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

places.name: Cathedral Lakes Trail
places.address: Cathedral Lakes Trailhead, Tioga Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: NPS paved lot; fills early; wilderness permit required for overnight

activities.notes: 7-mile round trip to Lower Cathedral Lake (9,200 ft). Steep initial 3 miles. Stunning reflection of Cathedral Peak. Camping available with permit. Continue 1 mile to Upper Lake for solitude.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

places.name: Dana Lakes and Mount Dana
places.address: Tioga Pass Entrance Station area, Tioga Rd (Hwy 120), Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Park at pass entrance area or along road; east side of park boundary

activities.notes: 6.2-mile round trip to 13,061 ft Mount Dana, Yosemite's second highest peak. Trail starts at 9,943 ft (Tioga Pass). Class 1-2 scramble. Breathtaking views of Mono Lake. Start before 7am.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

places.name: Dog Lake Trail
places.parking_notes: Park at the Tuolumne Meadows Lodge parking area or the Lembert Dome / Dog Lake trailhead (free, off Tioga Rd east of Tuolumne Meadows Visitor Center). Arrive early — lots fill by 9am in summer.

activities.notes: Short 2.2-mile round trip through lodgepole pines and meadow to a pretty subalpine lake. Steady but manageable climb (~400 ft) then flat approach to the lake. Excellent for kids comfortable with a bit of uphill. Quiet compared to other Tuolumne destinations. Swimming possible in the lake. Wildflowers in July–August.

Short 2.2-mile round trip through lodgepole pines and meadow to a pretty subalpine lake. Steady but manageable climb (~400 ft) then flat approach to the lake. Excellent for kids comfortable with a bit of uphill. Quiet compared to other Tuolumne destinations. Swimming possible in the lake. Wildflowers in July–August.

Round trip to Dog Lake from the Lembert Dome trailhead. Classic Tuolumne half-day walk through high-elevation lodgepole forest. The lake sits in a glacially carved basin at 9,170 ft with views toward the Cathedral Range. Can be combined with the Lembert Dome area for a full half-day.

Round trip to Dog Lake from the Lembert Dome trailhead. Classic Tuolumne half-day walk through high-elevation lodgepole forest. The lake sits in a glacially carved basin at 9,170 ft with views toward the Cathedral Range. Can be combined with Lembert Dome area for a full half-day.

places.name: Dog Lake Trailhead
places.address: Dog Lake Parking, Tuolumne Meadows, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Adjacent to Lembert Dome lot, north of Tioga Rd. Overnight quota: 12 lottery / 8 first-come.

activities.notes: Dog Lake (9,170 ft): shallow, warm-for-Sierra swimming lake. Can be combined with Lembert Dome for 6.1 km loop. Forested trail, less exposed on stormy days. Good on days with afternoon thunderstorm risk.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

places.name: Glen Aulin Trailhead — Lembert Dome
places.address: Lembert Dome Parking, Tuolumne Meadows, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Large lot, north side Tioga Rd. Free with park entry. Flush toilets. Also summer shuttle stop.

activities.notes: Lembert Dome base bouldering on world-class Tuolumne granite. NPS: 'pinching crystals on sun-drenched Tuolumne Meadows domes.' Slab climbing and established problems. Helmets recommended. No permit needed.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; rock can overheat midday in August

activities.notes: Classic NPS hike: 'Follow the Tuolumne River 5.5 miles as it drops to Glen Aulin.' Passes Tuolumne Falls and White Cascade. Glen Aulin HSC at end. Optional extension to Waterwheel Falls (+3 miles).
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

activities.notes: Lembert Dome summit hike: 3.8-mile round trip via Dog Lake trail with steep final approach on open granite to summit at 9,450 ft. Panoramic views of meadows and peaks. NPS: 'Stay off domes during thunderstorms.'
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

activities.notes: Glen Aulin trail run: JMT/PCT singletrack follows Tuolumne River 8.9 km to Glen Aulin HSC and back. Net downhill going (240 m gain on return). Passes Tuolumne Falls and White Cascade. Elevation 8,600–7,800 ft.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

activities.notes: Young Lakes Loop trail run via Glen Aulin or Dog Lake. NPS lists as 13.6-mile day hike. Classic trail running objective reaching Young Lakes at 10,000 ft.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

places.name: John Muir Trail Run - Tuolumne Section
places.address: Tuolumne Meadows JMT trailhead, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Park at wilderness center lot; bear canister required for overnight; wilderness permit needed

activities.notes: 12-mile out-and-back on JMT/PCT from Tuolumne Meadows to Cathedral Lakes junction. Classic Sierra high country running. Mix of meadow and forest. Elevation 8,600-9,400 ft.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

places.name: Lembert Dome Trail
places.address: Lembert Dome parking, Tioga Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Large paved NPS lot; free with park entrance; fills by 9am weekends

activities.notes: 2.8-mile round trip with Class 2 scramble to 9,450 ft summit. 360° views of Tuolumne Meadows, Sierra Crest. Exposed summit—use caution with children. Glacial erratics.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

places.name: Marmot Dome via Young Lakes Trail
places.address: Tuolumne Meadows Wilderness Center, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Wilderness center lot; free with park entrance; wilderness permit needed if continuing to Young Lakes

activities.notes: 4-mile out-and-back to Marmot Dome (9,800 ft) on way to Young Lakes. Cross-country last 0.3 miles but obvious route. Class 1-2. Views of Tuolumne Meadows and surrounding peaks. Good practice for cross-country navigation. Continue to Young Lakes for full day.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — off exposed terrain by noon

places.name: Mono Pass Trailhead — Dana Meadows
places.address: Mono Pass Trailhead, Tioga Rd at Dana Meadows, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Small roadside parking on Tioga Rd ~1.5 mi west of Tioga Pass. Limited spaces. Overnight quota: 9 lottery / 6 first-come.

activities.notes: Mono Pass: 'Climb steadily for views of Mono Lake and eastern Sierra.' Trail gains 300 m to Mono Pass at 10,604 ft on Yosemite boundary. Historic mining-era cabins at pass. Views east to Mono Lake and Great Basin.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

places.name: Olmsted Point Viewpoint
places.address: Olmsted Point Parking, Tioga Rd, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Large pullout, south side Tioga Rd. Restrooms on site. Free with park entry.

activities.notes: Glacier-polished slabs and large erratics offer informal scrambling immediately from parking. Natural beginner granite environment. No established problems. NPS geology interpretation on site.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; rock can overheat midday in August

activities.notes: Kids enjoy exploring glacial erratic boulders. Educational NPS geology signage on site. Good photo stop.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

activities.notes: Short informal walk across glacier-polished granite to viewpoint. Half Dome visible. Glacial erratics scattered across slabs. No maintained trail.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

places.name: Parsons Lodge & Soda Springs
places.address: Tuolumne Meadows, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Lembert Dome lot or VC lot, free with park entry

activities.notes: NPS top family pick: kids love touching carbonated spring water. Mostly flat, stroller-accessible. Bring sunscreen at 8,600 ft.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

activities.notes: Flat stroll to carbonated Soda Springs and historic Parsons Memorial Lodge. Where Muir and Johnson conceived Yosemite NP. Unpaved, well-maintained.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

places.name: Parsons Memorial Lodge Loop
places.address: Lembert Dome parking area, Tioga Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Lembert Dome lot; free with park entrance; ADA accessible route available

activities.notes: 1-mile easy loop on old mining road to Parsons Lodge (Sierra Club-built, 1915). Soda Springs, boardwalks. Interpretive programs some days. Excellent for children—nature at eye level.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

places.name: Pothole Dome
places.address: Pothole Dome Pullout, Tioga Rd, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Small roadside pullout, ~10 cars, south side Tioga Rd west end of meadow

activities.notes: Low-angle exfoliation dome — beginner granite friction. Potholes (glacial melt features) at base. Good family intro to Tuolumne granite. No permit needed.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; rock can overheat midday in August

activities.notes: NPS-listed short family option. Easy scramble for kids 5+. Granite friction — closed-toe shoes recommended.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

activities.notes: Short walk then easy granite scramble. First panoramic view of Tuolumne Meadows from summit. Slippery when wet.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

places.name: Pothole Dome Summit
places.address: Pothole Dome pullout, Tioga Rd (Hwy 120), Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Small pullout on Tioga Road; free with park entrance

activities.notes: 1.2-mile round trip to granite dome summit at 8,700 ft. Class 1 walking—no real scrambling. Tuolumne River views, meadow panorama. Perfect first dome for kids/families. Can link with meadow walk for longer day.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — off exposed terrain by noon

places.name: Pywiack Dome — Tuolumne Bouldering
places.address: Tenaya Lake East Parking, Tioga Rd (Hwy 120), Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: East Tenaya Lake picnic lot on Tioga Rd. Free with park entrance fee. 0.4 mi walk to Pywiack Dome base. Summer shuttle stop nearby.

activities.notes: Pywiack Dome is a glacier-polished granite dome east of Tenaya Lake in Tuolumne Meadows with accessible low-angle slab bouldering at its base. Characteristic Tuolumne friction problems on exfoliated granite at 8,150 ft. Grades V0–V4 on clean, sun-warmed slabs. Often combined with Tenaya Lake swimming. NPS rock-climbing regulations apply; no permit needed for bouldering at base. Best season July–September. Part of the same high-altitude dome cluster as Lembert and Pothole Dome.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; rock can overheat midday in August

places.name: Rafferty Creek / Wilderness Center Trailhead
places.address: Wilderness Center Parking, Tuolumne Meadows, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Wilderness Center lot, north of Tioga Rd. Overnight quota: 12 lottery / 8 first-come (Rafferty Creek).

activities.notes: Vogelsang via Rafferty Creek: long day hike to Vogelsang HSC at 10,330 ft. 570 m gain into high alpine zone. Stunning Clark Range and Cathedral Range views. Wilderness permit for overnight. Classic backpacking route.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

places.name: River Loop via Pothole Dome
places.address: Pothole Dome pullout, Tioga Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Small dirt pullout on west side Tioga Road; limited spaces—arrive early

activities.notes: 1.5-mile round trip to granite dome summit with Tuolumne River views. Easy scramble (Class 1). Glacial polish. Wildflowers in cracks. Gentle introduction to Sierra scrambling.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

places.name: Soda Springs Historic Site Walk
places.address: Soda Springs, Tuolumne Meadows, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: NPS paved lot at Lembert Dome trailhead; Tioga Road seasonal closure Nov-May; fills weekends

activities.notes: 1.2-mile round trip on old road bed to carbonated mineral springs and historic Parsons Memorial Lodge. ADA-accessible boardwalk. Interpretive signs. One of Tuolumne's easiest walks at 8,600 ft.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

places.name: Tenaya Lake Shore
places.address: Tenaya Lake West Picnic Area, Tioga Rd, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Two picnic lots (east/west). West lot wheelchair accessible. Both free with park entry. Fills by mid-morning weekends.

activities.notes: Flat shoreline walk with sandy beaches — one of Yosemite's best family swimming spots. Water cold even in July. Accessible picnic tables.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

activities.notes: Point-to-point trail run, Tenaya Lake east to Tuolumne Meadows. Mix of use trails and Tioga Rd shoulder. Elevation 8,150–8,600 ft. Car shuttle or park shuttle needed for one-way.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

activities.notes: Informal trail circling Tenaya Lake at 8,150 ft. Swimming, paddleboarding, kayaking from shore. No permit required.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

places.name: Tenaya Lake Trailhead - High Country Running
places.address: Tenaya Lake, Tioga Rd (Hwy 120), Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: NPS day-use lots; free with park entrance; multiple access points

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 10K-30K TECHNICAL ALPINE] 8,150 ft alpine lake trailhead. Connects to Clouds Rest trail, Sunrise Lakes, Half Dome approach. 10K Sunrise Lakes loop, 16K to Clouds Rest junction, 30K+ Half Dome summit return. Technical rocky sections. Dramatic high country. Snow patches into July. Serious mountain running requiring preparation.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

places.name: Tioga Pass Entrance Trailhead
places.address: Tioga Pass Entrance Station, Hwy 120, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Small pullout inside Tioga Pass entrance, ~20 cars. Often full summer mornings. Highest trailhead in park at 9,945 ft.

activities.notes: Gaylor Lakes: 'Climb steadily to ridge with spectacular views of Dana Meadows.' Middle Gaylor Lake then optional cross-country to Great Sierra Mine ruins. Start at 9,945 ft. Best short high-altitude hike in Yosemite.
activities.conditions_note: Highest paved road crossing in Sierra; typically open July–October

activities.notes: Mount Dana (13,061 ft): 2nd highest peak in Yosemite. ~9.7 km RT with 945 m gain, all above 9,945 ft. No maintained trail — cross-country on use trail. Outstanding views of Mono Lake and Sierra crest. Afternoon thunderstorms July–Aug.
activities.conditions_note: Highest paved road crossing in Sierra; typically open July–October

places.name: Tuolumne High Country Domes — Bouldering
places.address: Various Tioga Rd pullouts, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Fairview Dome pullout at ~37.860, -119.400. Daff Dome and Stately Pleasure Dome from nearby pullouts. Cathedral Peak bouldering from Cathedral Lakes trailhead.

activities.notes: World-renowned high-altitude granite bouldering. Established problems at Fairview Dome base (grades V0–V8), Daff Dome, Stately Pleasure Dome. NPS: 'pinching crystals on sun-drenched Tuolumne domes.' Best season July–Sept. NPS climbing regs apply. No permit for bouldering.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; rock can overheat midday in August

places.name: Tuolumne Meadows Bouldering - Puppy Dome
places.address: Puppy Dome, Tioga Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Dirt pullout on Tioga Road near mile marker; small area—car shuttle recommended

activities.notes: Granite bouldering at 8,700 ft. Classics: 'Bachar-Yerian' (V2), 'Nemesis' (V6). Knobby granite. Best July-September. Limited landings—bring multiple pads. Part of famous Camp 4-style scene.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; rock can overheat midday in August

places.name: Tuolumne Meadows Loop
places.address: Tuolumne Meadows, Tioga Rd (Hwy 120), Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Multiple NPS lots along Tioga Road; free with park entrance ($35/vehicle); fills by 10am summer

activities.notes: 2.5-mile gravel path along Tuolumne River through subalpine meadow. PCT/JMT junction area. Wildflowers July-August. River access. Generally flat with minimal elevation change.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

places.name: Tuolumne Meadows Trail Running Epicenter
places.address: Tuolumne Meadows Wilderness Center, Tioga Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: NPS gravel lots; $35 park entrance; fills by 9am; seasonal June-Oct

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-42K+ HIGH ALPINE] World-class high-altitude running at 8,600 ft. PCT/JMT corridor access. 5K meadow loop, 10K to Soda Springs, 21K to Lyell Canyon bridge, 42K+ to Donohue Pass. Gentle grades, perfect tread. Must-do for any serious mountain runner. Acclimatization essential—don't sprint first day. Afternoon thunderstorms rule.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

places.name: Tuolumne Meadows Visitor Center Trailhead
places.address: Tuolumne Meadows Visitor Center, 14000 Tioga Rd, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: VC lot, south side Tioga Rd. Free with park entry. Summer shuttle stop. Can fill by 8 AM weekends.

activities.notes: Cathedral Lakes: NPS 'most popular trail climbs steadily to Upper Cathedral Lake.' Lower at 9,288 ft, Upper at 9,585 ft beneath Cathedral Peak (10,911 ft). Wildflowers peak late July. Wilderness permit for overnight.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

activities.notes: Elizabeth Lake: 'Glacier-carved lake at the base of Unicorn Peak' (9,503 ft). Steady climb over first 2 miles. Less crowded than Cathedral Lakes. Optional 1-mile loop around lake. No day use permit needed.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; afternoon thunderstorms Jul–Aug — summit by noon

activities.notes: Informal meadow loop combining meadow paths, Tuolumne River corridor, Soda Springs, Parsons Lodge. Nearly flat at 8,600 ft. Popular with lodge guests. Mix of dirt trail and grass — muddy early season.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

activities.notes: Yosemite Half Marathon: annual road/trail event utilizing Tioga Rd and Tuolumne Meadows. High altitude racing at 8,600 ft. Verify 2026 date and NPS permit status at recreation.gov.
activities.conditions_note: Annual event — verify current dates and registration before planning your trip

places.name: Tuolumne Meadows to Lyell Canyon
places.address: Tuolumne Meadows Wilderness Center, Tioga Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: NPS gravel lot at wilderness center; free with park entrance; trailhead busiest in park

activities.notes: 6.2-mile out-and-back along Tuolumne River into Lyell Canyon. Gradual 200 ft gain on PCT/JMT tread. World-class trail running at 8,600-8,900 ft. Best conditions July-September.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

places.name: Tuolumne River Walk — Lyell Fork to Twin Bridges
places.address: Dog Lake Trailhead, Tuolumne Meadows, Yosemite NP, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Dog Lake lot, north side Tioga Rd east of VC. Free. Overflow at Wilderness Center.

activities.notes: Turn-around version for families. Easy wading access at multiple points. Marmots and deer frequently seen.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

activities.notes: One of the finest high-altitude trail runs in the Sierra. Flat runnable JMT/PCT corridor to Lyell Base Camp (~9.7 km one-way). Elevation 8,700–9,500 ft. Wilderness permit for overnight; day use free.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road closes November–May; start early to avoid afternoon storms

activities.notes: Flat JMT along Lyell Fork to Twin Bridges — scenic wading spot. Mostly flat, uneven terrain. Excellent early-morning light on river.
activities.conditions_note: Tioga Road typically closes November–May; verify at nps.gov/yose

zones.name: Mammoth Lakes
zones.description: Major Eastern Sierra resort town at 7,800 ft with year-round tourism. Home to Mammoth Mountain ski area, Devils Postpile National Monument, Inyo National Forest trail networks, June Mountain, and proximity to the JMT southern terminus area.

places.name: Barrett Lake Trail
places.address: Lake George Campground Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: USFS Lake George parking lot; fee in season

activities.notes: 4-mile round trip from Lake George to Barrett Lake (9,648 ft) and optional TJ Lake. Off-trail scramble section to TJ Lake. Quieter than Crystal Lake trail. Spectacular views. Best July–Oct.

places.name: Convict Lake Loop
places.address: Convict Lake Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free large parking lot at Convict Lake Resort; pit toilets and flush restrooms at resort; no fee for day use; boat rentals available in summer

activities.notes: Partially shaded loop around Convict Lake at 7,583 ft with dramatic views of 12,000+ ft peaks including Laurel and Bloody mountains. Rocky talus on north shore section is moderately challenging for small children. Inyo NF managed. Fishing, picnicking, and horse rentals at resort.

places.name: Convict Lake Loop Walk
places.address: Convict Lake Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free paved parking at Convict Lake day-use area and marina; ADA spaces available

activities.notes: 2-mile loop hugging Convict Lake shoreline. ADA-accessible paved section between marina and parking. Boardwalk bridge over Convict Creek. Short hill on northeast shore. Popular with families and anglers. Dramatic granite walls of Mt. Morrison overhead.

places.name: Convict Lake Loop — Family Walk
places.address: Convict Lake Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free paved parking at Convict Lake day-use area; ADA spaces at marina

activities.notes: Same loop as walk entry; highlighted here for family audiences. ADA-accessible paved section. Fishing, picnicking, and wildlife watching. Excellent for young children. Classic Mammoth family outing.

places.name: Convict Lake to Convict Creek Canyon
places.address: Convict Lake Resort, 2000 Convict Lake Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free paved parking at Convict Lake Resort; flush restrooms at resort; no day-use fee; popular spot — arrive before 9 AM on summer weekends
