activities.notes: 8-mile round trip from Convict Lake (7,583 ft) up Convict Creek Canyon into the John Muir Wilderness via Inyo NF. Dramatic granite walls and canyon scenery. Trail follows creek upstream toward Mildred and Dorothy lakes. Can be done as an out-and-back to Lake Genevieve or extended further into the wilderness.

places.name: Convict Lake to Mildred Lake (Convict Creek Trail)
places.address: Convict Lake Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free paved lot at Convict Lake; day-use parking west end of lake

activities.notes: 7.1-mile trail along Convict Creek into John Muir Wilderness. Passes Mildred Lake at 5 miles with option to continue to Lake Dorothy (10,275 ft), Lake Genevieve, and Edith Lake. Dramatic canyon scenery. Bears active—canister required overnight.

places.name: Crystal Crag Peak
places.address: Lake George Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Lake George area; USFS day use; $5 fee summer

activities.notes: 5-mile round trip to 10,377 ft via Duck Pass Trail then fork to Crystal Lake/Crag. Moderately steep forest trail, then talus field to summit block. Class 2 scrambling on granite final 100 yards. Not technical but requires route-finding. Views of Mammoth Lakes basin.
activities.conditions_note: Start early Jul–Aug; afternoon thunderstorms common above treeline

places.name: Crystal Lake Trail
places.address: Lake George Campground Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: USFS Lake George parking lot; fee in season

activities.notes: 3.5-mile round trip from Lake George to Crystal Lake (9,512 ft) below Crystal Crag. Steady switchback climb through lodgepole. Glacially scoured basin. Moderately challenging. Best July–Oct. Wilderness permit not required for day hike.

places.name: Deadman Summit Bouldering
places.address: Deadman Summit, Hwy 395 / June Lake Loop Rd, CA 93529
places.parking_notes: Pullouts off Hwy 395 near Deadman Summit (8,041 ft); free

activities.notes: Volcanic and rhyolite bouldering near Deadman Creek drainage ~15 miles north of Mammoth on Hwy 395. Multiple scattered boulder fields; some problems V0–V7. Extended season—often accessible when Lakes Basin snowbound. Inyo National Forest.

places.name: Deadman Summit Boulders
places.address: US-395 at Deadman Summit, Lee Vining, CA 93541
places.parking_notes: Pullout parking on US-395 near Deadman Summit; free; no facilities; exposed high-desert location at ~8,000 ft; drive ~15 min north of Mammoth Lakes on US-395

activities.notes: Volcanic tuff and rhyolite boulders at Deadman Summit on Inyo NF land. Less-visited area with problems V0–V6 on quality volcanic rock. Good extended-season option (often climbable April–November) due to lower elevation. Roadside access makes this a quick stop for climbers passing on US-395.

places.name: Devils Postpile Formation Trail
places.address: Devils Postpile Trailhead, Reds Meadow Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Same as Rainbow Falls — mandatory shuttle mid-June through Labor Day; NPS entrance fee included in shuttle pass; ranger station and restrooms at trailhead

activities.notes: 0.8-mile round trip to the base of the Devils Postpile columnar basalt formation; columns reach 60 feet tall. Optional 15-minute uphill scramble adds ~0.5 mile to reach the top for mosaic views. NPS-managed. Excellent geology education opportunity for children.
activities.conditions_note: Mandatory shuttle from Mammoth Mountain required June–September

places.name: Devils Postpile Formation Walk
places.address: Devils Postpile National Monument, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Shuttle access only mid-June–early Sept (stops 6–7 near ranger station). Fee: $8–15/person shuttle. Private vehicles permitted off-season.

activities.notes: Easy 0.8-mile round trip to base of 60-ft columnar basalt columns; add 15-min uphill spur to summit view with glacial polish. One of world's finest examples of columnar basalt. Excellent for all ages.
activities.conditions_note: Mandatory shuttle from Mammoth Mountain required June–September

places.name: Devils Postpile Walk — Family
places.address: Devils Postpile National Monument, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Shuttle only mid-June–early Sept; fee $8–15/person

activities.notes: Fun geology lesson—hexagonal basalt columns fascinate kids. Very short, very easy. Junior Ranger program available at visitor center. Top of columns accessible via short uphill spur.
activities.conditions_note: Mandatory shuttle from Mammoth Mountain required June–September

places.name: Duck Pass Trail
places.address: Lake George Campground Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: USFS Lake George parking lot; fee in season. Trailhead at campground entrance.

activities.notes: 9 miles round trip to Duck Pass (10,797 ft) via Arrowhead, Skelton, and Horse lakes. Sustained climb through John Muir Wilderness. Stunning high alpine terrain. Wilderness permit required for overnight. Best July–Oct.

places.name: Earthquake Fault Area Bouldering
places.address: Earthquake Fault Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free paved parking lot at Earthquake Fault geological site on Minaret Rd; pit toilets; Inyo NF interpretive area; no fee

activities.notes: Volcanic rhyolite boulders around the Earthquake Fault geological site on Inyo NF land near Mammoth Mountain. The fault itself (a fissure crack) is the primary attraction but boulders in the vicinity offer V0–V4 problems. Excellent combination with the free geological interpretive walk. Good intro area for beginners.

places.name: Horseshoe Lake Shoreline Loop
places.address: Horseshoe Lake Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free paved parking area at Horseshoe Lake; no fee; pit toilets available; note CO2 tree-kill zone nearby (Mammoth Mountain volcanic area) — stay on trail

activities.notes: Easy flat loop around Horseshoe Lake at 8,900 ft — the highest of the Mammoth Lakes Basin lakes accessible by road. Sandy beaches, clear water, volcanic pumice shoreline. Note the bleached-out area of tree kill from volcanic CO2 seepage (interpretive signs explain). Short and accessible for all ages.

Easy flat loop around Horseshoe Lake at 8,900 ft — the highest lake in the Mammoth Lakes Basin accessible by road. Sandy beaches, clear water, volcanic pumice shoreline. Interpretive signs explain the CO2-caused tree kill zone. Short and accessible for all ages.

Flat, forested loop around Horseshoe Lake at 8,928 ft. Sandy pumice soil, good fishing access, picnic areas. Part of Inyo National Forest Mammoth Lakes Basin. Tree-kill area caused by volcanic CO2 gas is visible and interpretive signs explain the phenomenon.

places.name: Hot Creek Geological Site Walk
places.address: Hot Creek Hatchery Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free paved lot at canyon rim; vault toilets on site

activities.notes: Paved 0.2-mi path descends 100 ft to geothermal pools. Swimming and bathing prohibited. Stay on walkways. Closed sunset–sunrise. Extraordinary geology; excellent for all ages with supervision.
activities.conditions_note: Swimming prohibited since 2006; geothermal steam most visible on cold mornings

places.name: Hot Creek Geological Site — Family Walk
places.address: Hot Creek Hatchery Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free paved lot at canyon rim

activities.notes: Paved path to geothermal hot springs and boiling pools. Great educational stop for kids—volcanic geology visible. Adult supervision required near thermal hazards. No swimming.
activities.conditions_note: Swimming prohibited since 2006; geothermal steam most visible on cold mornings

places.name: Lake George Area Bouldering
places.address: Lake George Trailhead, Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Lake George parking area off Lake Mary Rd; free; pit toilets; same lot as Crystal Lake and Mammoth Crest trails; approach boulders from south shore of lake

activities.notes: Granite boulders in the Lake George basin at ~9,000 ft on Inyo NF land. Problems range V1–V7 on clean Sierra granite. Short approach. Often combined with hiking Crystal Lake or Mammoth Crest. Season typically July–October due to snowpack at this elevation. Stunning alpine setting below Crystal Crag.

places.name: Lake George to Crystal Crag Overlook
places.address: Lake George Campground Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: USFS Lake George parking lot

activities.notes: Extended Lakes Basin hike combining Crystal Lake trail with ridge scramble to Crystal Crag saddle (10,377 ft) for panoramic views of Mammoth Lakes Basin. Uses Crystal Lake trail then climbs cross-country. Best for experienced hikers.

places.name: Lake George to Lake Mary Walk
places.address: Lake George Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Paved lot at Lake George Campground (USFS); fee area in season

activities.notes: Short connector walk between Lake George (9,000 ft) and Lake Mary (8,895 ft) through lodgepole pine. Lakeside scenery, gentle grades. Good warm-up walk in the basin.

places.name: Lake George — Family Walk
places.address: Lake George Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: USFS paved lot at Lake George Campground; fee in season

activities.notes: Short lakeside walk around Lake George at 9,000 ft. Beautiful views of Crystal Crag. Fishing access. Very accessible for young children.

places.name: Lake Mary Lakeside Path
places.address: Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Large free parking area at Lake Mary boat launch / campground; pit toilets; fishing pier; arrives off Lake Mary Rd from Mammoth Lakes Basin. Can be busy on summer weekends.

activities.notes: Gentle path along Lake Mary shoreline at 8,890 ft. Largest lake in Mammoth Lakes Basin. Easy terrain suitable for strollers on the paved sections; gravel near campgrounds. Beautiful views of Mammoth Crest. Inyo NF managed. Fishing, kayak rentals nearby.

places.name: Lake Mary Loop Walk
places.address: Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Large USFS paved lot at Lake Mary Campground; fee may apply in season

activities.notes: Flat loop around Lake Mary at 8,895 ft elevation. Paved and dirt sections. Stunning alpine scenery, good fishing access. Part of Mammoth Lakes Basin.

places.name: Lake Mary Shoreline Walk — Family
places.address: Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: USFS paved lot at Lake Mary; fee in season

activities.notes: Flat loop around scenic alpine lake at 8,895 ft. Good fishing and picnicking. Restrooms at campground. Crowded on summer weekends; arrive early.

places.name: Lakes Basin Bouldering (Lake George / Lake Mary area)
places.address: Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: USFS Lake Mary or Lake George parking lots

activities.notes: Granite boulders scattered around Lakes Basin near Lake George and Lake Mary at 8,900–9,200 ft. High-quality Sierra granite. Problems V0–V8. Short approach from parking. Inyo National Forest land. Best July–Oct when snow-free.

places.name: Lakes Basin Trail Complex - Main Trailhead
places.address: Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546 (Lake Mary Campground area)
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lots $5; 5 trailheads in 2-mile stretch; seasonal June-Oct

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-30K+ ALPINE LAKES] 7 alpine lakes trail network. 5K Lake Mary loop, 10K Mary+George, 18K Duck Pass, 30K+ to PCT junction. 8,900-10,500 ft. Granite and aspen. Water sources available. Multi-loop options. Trail running paradise. Active bear population—follow food storage rules. Afternoon T-storms July-Aug.

places.name: Laurel Lakes / Edith Lake Trail
places.address: Laurel Lakes 4x4 Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Dirt pullout at top of Laurel Lakes 4x4 road (high-clearance vehicle required for road); park at bottom and walk up if needed

activities.notes: 4.2-mile trail starting at ~9,910 ft. Immediately enters John Muir Wilderness. Passes through dramatic terrain to Edith Lake. Optional extension to Lake Genevieve and Lake Dorothy. Less-traveled alternative to Lakes Basin trails.

places.name: Mammoth Crest Trail
places.address: Lake George Trailhead, Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Lake George parking area off Lake Mary Rd; same lot as Crystal Lake trail; free; pit toilets; exposed crest trail — check weather, bring layers

activities.notes: 7-mile round trip from Lake George TH to Mammoth Crest (10,400 ft) via Crystal Lake. Exposed ridgeline with sweeping views of Long Valley Caldera, Mono Lake, and the Minarets. Class 2 terrain on upper sections. Inyo National Forest. Best July–September when snow-free.

places.name: Mammoth Lakes Basin Bike Path (Lake Mary to Town)
places.address: Old Mammoth Rd & Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Multiple pullouts along Lake Mary Rd; free street parking in town

activities.notes: Paved multi-use path connecting Mammoth Lakes town center to Lakes Basin area. Ideal for strollers, casual walkers, and cyclists. Gentle grades. Town of Mammoth Lakes maintained.

places.name: Mammoth Lakes Basin Road Run — Lakes Basin Loop
places.address: Old Mammoth Rd & Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Street parking at Old Mammoth Rd; Mammoth Creek Park has free lot; no trailhead fee; suitable as year-round training route on road and packed gravel

activities.notes: Popular local training route from town up Lake Mary Rd through Mammoth Lakes Basin visiting Twin Lakes, Lake Mary, Horseshoe Lake and back. Combination of paved road, bike path, and dirt trail. High-altitude training at 8,000–9,000 ft. Used by local running community and as prep for Mammoth Trail Fest races.

places.name: Mammoth Lakes Basin Trail Run Route (Lakes Basin Loop)
places.address: Lake Mary Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: USFS Lake Mary parking lot

activities.notes: Popular local training route linking Lake Mary, Lake George, Crystal Lake, Horseshoe Lake, and Mammoth Pass. Mix of singletrack and fire road. Altitude 8,900–9,700 ft. Non-motorized summer trail. No designated race—local running route.

places.name: Mammoth Lakes Trail System — Lakes Basin Path (Paved Multi-Use)
places.address: Village Dr, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Multiple access points; Mammoth Village parking structure (free first 2 hours); no dedicated trailhead lot but ample street and lot parking throughout town; accessible year-round

activities.notes: Paved and compacted-gravel multi-use path connecting Mammoth Village toward Lakes Basin. Shared with cyclists and strollers. Passes through Shady Rest Park and connects to Mammoth Creek Park. Inyo NF manages portions through Lakes Basin corridor. Suitable for all ages and abilities.

places.name: Mammoth Mountain Trail Running Base
places.address: Mammoth Mountain Main Lodge, 10001 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Main lodge lots; $10-25/day; gondola access for downhill runs

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-42K SKI RESORT TRAILS] Summer use of ski resort trail network. 5K switchback loops, 10K to Panorama Dome, 21K to Reds Meadow, 42K point-to-point to Devils Postpile. Marked summer trails. 7,800-11,000 ft. Chairlift up/run down options. Mammoth Trail Festival race course. Excellent training at altitude. Café/amenities at lodge.

places.name: Mammoth Rock (West Slope)
places.address: Mammoth Rock Trail, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Old Mammoth Rd access; trailhead parking limited

activities.notes: 2.5-mile round trip to 9,400 ft volcanic rock summit. Western approach is walkable—eastern side has cliffs to avoid. Good views of Mammoth Mountain. Trail fades near top but cairns mark route. Used for both hiking and bouldering access.
activities.conditions_note: Start early Jul–Aug; afternoon thunderstorms common above treeline

places.name: Mammoth Rock Bouldering Area
places.address: Mammoth Rock Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Small dirt lot off Old Mammoth Rd near Mammoth Rock Trail; free; walk ~5 min to boulders

activities.notes: Volcanic rhyolite bouldering at ~8,000 ft on the slopes below Mammoth Mountain. Problems range V0–V7. Best in summer and fall when lowland crags are too hot. Short approach. Multiple sectors across the hillside. Inyo National Forest land.

places.name: Mammoth Trail Fest — Dragon's Back Ascent (Hill Climb)
places.address: 163 Twin Lakes Rd (Tamarack Lodge Yurt), Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Shuttle provided from Village Plaza; no self-parking at start required

activities.notes: 3 miles / 2,400 ft vertical straight up Mammoth Mountain to 11,053-ft summit. Technical singletrack at high altitude. Orange flags. Annual race event, September. Free gondola descent after. Prize purse $3,400.
activities.conditions_note: Annual July event — verify schedule at mammothtrailfest.com

places.name: Mammoth Trail Fest — Mammoth 26K
places.address: Village at Mammoth, 6201 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Village parking structures; ride-share recommended on race day

activities.notes: 16.1 miles / 3,900+ ft gain. Elevation range 8,050–11,053 ft. Single loop, no reused trail. Singletrack, pumice, rocky alpine terrain, summit of Mammoth Mountain. Annual event Sept (Sunday). 7-hr cutoff; 97% finish rate in 2025.
activities.conditions_note: Annual July event — verify schedule at mammothtrailfest.com

places.name: Mammoth Trail Fest — Mammoth 50K
places.address: Village at Mammoth, 6201 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Village parking structures; ride-share recommended on race day

activities.notes: 32 miles with ~7,000 ft gain. Avg elevation 9,100 ft; lowest 8,050 ft; highest 11,053 ft. Single loop—no out-and-backs. Singletrack, pumice, bike path, seven alpine lakes. Finishes at Village after Dragon's Back summit. Annual event Sept (Saturday). 12-hr cutoff.
activities.conditions_note: Annual July event — verify schedule at mammothtrailfest.com

places.name: Mammoth Trail Fest — Mammoth Triple
places.address: Village Plaza, 6201 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Village at Mammoth area; multi-day event Sept 25–27; race communications provide daily parking/shuttle guidance

activities.notes: Three-day stage event combining Dragon's Back Ascent (4.8 km / 2,400 ft gain), Mammoth 50K (51.5 km / 7,000 ft gain), and Mammoth 26K (26 km / 3,900 ft gain) — totaling ~51 miles / 82 km and 13,400+ ft of vertical gain across one weekend in September.
activities.conditions_note: Annual July event — verify schedule at mammothtrailfest.com

places.name: Mammoth Trail Fest — Mammoth Triple (50K + 26K + DBA)
places.address: Village at Mammoth, 6201 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Village parking structures; multi-day event

activities.notes: 3-day stage race: Dragon's Back Ascent (Fri) + 50K (Sat) + 26K (Sun). Total 51 miles / 13,400+ ft gain. Golden Trail Series event. Top-3 per category awards at Village Plaza on Sunday. Random drug testing applies.
activities.conditions_note: Annual July event — verify schedule at mammothtrailfest.com

places.name: Mammoth Trail Fest — Tenkay (10K Night Race)
places.address: Village Plaza, 6201 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Village at Mammoth parking; evening start means less competition for parking; event starts at 6:45 PM

activities.notes: Evening single-loop trail race (~8.5 km / 5.3 miles) with ~800 ft elevation gain/loss. Uptown Trail ascent to Party Summit aid station then Downtown Trail descent. Disco lights in trees illuminate the course. Starts at 6:45 PM at Village Plaza. Part of Mammoth Trail Fest weekend in September.
activities.conditions_note: Annual July event — verify schedule at mammothtrailfest.com

places.name: Mammoth Trail Fest — Tenkay 10K (Night Run)
places.address: Village at Mammoth, 6201 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Village parking structures

activities.notes: ~5.9 miles; ~800 ft gain/loss. After-dark party race at 6:45 PM (Friday). Climbs Uptown Trail to summit, descends Downtown Trail. Marked with green flags and reflective tape, disco lights at aid stations. 'Perfect entry level event.' Cutoff 9 PM.
activities.conditions_note: Annual July event — verify schedule at mammothtrailfest.com

places.name: Minaret Falls Trail — Devils Postpile NM
places.address: Devils Postpile Trailhead, Reds Meadow Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Mandatory summer shuttle from Mammoth Mountain Adventure Center (same as Devils Postpile); restrooms at trailhead

activities.notes: Easy 1.4-mile round trip to Minaret Falls, a small cascading waterfall just outside the NM boundary along the San Joaquin River corridor. Flat to gently rolling. Good first hike for young children. Shaded riparian habitat. NPS Devils Postpile NM.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow June–July; significantly reduced by August

places.name: Minaret Falls Walk
places.address: Devils Postpile National Monument, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Shuttle access only mid-June–early Sept. Departs Mammoth Mountain Adventure Center.

activities.notes: Easy 1.4-mile round trip to small cascading waterfall just outside NM boundary. Forested trail alongside San Joaquin River. Short and family-appropriate.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow June–July; significantly reduced by August

places.name: Minaret Falls — Family Walk
places.address: Devils Postpile National Monument, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Shuttle access only mid-June–early Sept

activities.notes: Easy 1.4-mile round trip to small waterfall. Shaded forest trail alongside river. Short enough for toddlers with rest breaks. Great introduction to Reds Meadow Valley.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow June–July; significantly reduced by August

places.name: Rainbow Falls Trail (from Reds Meadow Trailhead)
places.address: Reds Meadow Rd, Devils Postpile NM, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Mandatory shuttle from Mammoth Mountain Adventure Center mid-June through early Sept; fee applies. Private vehicles with overnight camping permits or disabled placards may self-drive. Shuttle stops 9–10 nearest Rainbow Falls.

activities.notes: Rolling 5-mile (8 km) round-trip hike to 101-ft Rainbow Falls, tallest waterfall on Middle Fork San Joaquin River. Pumice trail through lodgepole forest. Can be hot and dry in summer—bring water. Return via shuttle stops 9 or 10. No pets on trail.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow June–July from snowmelt

places.name: Rainbow Falls Trail — Devils Postpile NM
places.address: Devils Postpile Trailhead, Reds Meadow Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Mandatory summer shuttle from Mammoth Mountain Adventure Center ($10/person unlimited rides, mid-June to Labor Day). Restrooms at trailhead near ranger station. Free parking at shuttle departure point.

activities.notes: 5-mile round-trip hike from Devils Postpile trailhead to Rainbow Falls, a 101-foot waterfall on the San Joaquin River. Rolling terrain. Bring water as trail can be hot and dry in summer. Managed by NPS Devils Postpile National Monument. Very popular with families; best visited early morning to avoid crowds.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow June–July from snowmelt

places.name: Rainbow Falls — Family Hike
places.address: Reds Meadow Rd, Devils Postpile NM, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Mandatory shuttle mid-June–early Sept from Mammoth Mountain Adventure Center

activities.notes: 101-ft waterfall on Middle Fork San Joaquin River. Moderately long for young children but the destination is spectacular. Rolling terrain on pumice trail. Shuttle return available at stops 9–10. Bring water—hot and sunny.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow June–July from snowmelt

places.name: Reds Meadow / Pumice Flat Bouldering
places.address: Reds Meadow Rd, Inyo National Forest, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Reds Meadow Resort parking area (shuttle stop 10) or Pumice Flat trailhead

activities.notes: Scattered granite and basalt boulders in the Reds Meadow valley near Devils Postpile. Access via mandatory shuttle mid-June–early Sept. Pumice soil approach. High-altitude (7,600 ft) alpine setting. V0–V6 range. Less crowded than town areas.
activities.conditions_note: Mandatory shuttle from Mammoth Mountain required June–September

places.name: Reds Meadow Area Loop Walk
places.address: Reds Meadow Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Access via mandatory shuttle from Mammoth Mountain Adventure Center mid-June to Labor Day ($10/person includes unlimited rides); limited vehicle access with permit before 7 AM or after 7 PM; portable restrooms at Reds Meadow Resort; small fee for resort day use

activities.notes: Loop through Reds Meadow Valley from Devils Postpile area to Reds Meadow Resort. Passes through meadow, hot springs pool, and forest. Devils Postpile NM / Inyo NF corridor. Hot springs swimming at Reds Meadow pool (fee). Rainbow Falls can be added as an extension.
activities.conditions_note: Mandatory shuttle from Mammoth Mountain required June–September

places.name: San Joaquin Mountain via Minaret Summit
places.address: Minaret Summit, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Minaret Vista pullout; free; seasonal closure Nov-May

activities.notes: 4.5-mile round trip to 11,600 ft via old road then cross-country ridge walk. Spectacular views of Ritter Range, Minarets, Banner/Ritter peaks. Walkable ridge—no scrambling. Good intro to cross-country travel with obvious goal. Windy on ridge.
activities.conditions_note: Start early Jul–Aug; afternoon thunderstorms common above treeline

places.name: Shady Rest Bouldering (Town Boulders)
places.address: Shady Rest Park, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Free parking at Shady Rest Park off Hwy 203; short walk to boulders in forest

activities.notes: In-town granite and volcanic bouldering scattered through the Jeffrey pine forest of Shady Rest Park/Inyo National Forest. Accessible year-round when not snowed in. Problems V0–V5. Popular local training area; relaxed atmosphere suitable for beginners and families.

places.name: Shady Rest Bouldering — Shady Rest Park
places.address: Shady Rest Park, Forest Trail Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Large free parking lot at Shady Rest Park / Mammoth Community Trail; no fee; flush restrooms in park; paved access; good cell signal

activities.notes: Scattered volcanic boulders in and around Shady Rest Park / Mammoth Community Park adjacent to Inyo NF. Beginner-friendly area with low-angle problems V0–V3. Very short walk from parking. Town setting; suitable for families with beginners. Best in spring and fall to avoid summer afternoon heat.

places.name: Shady Rest Park Running Hub
places.address: Shady Rest Park, Sawmill Cutoff Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Town park lot; free; restrooms; all-season access

activities.notes: Gentle flat paths through Mammoth's beloved Shady Rest Park — a 52-acre lodgepole pine forest in the heart of town. Wide packed-dirt and paved trails make easy loops through the trees. Great for strollers and small children. Picnic tables throughout. Connected to Mammoth's paved bike path network.

Gentle flat paths through Mammoth's beloved Shady Rest Park — a 52-acre lodgepole pine forest in the heart of town. Wide packed-dirt and paved trails make easy loops through the trees. Great for strollers and small children. Picnic tables throughout. Connected to Mammoth's paved bike path network.

[TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-15K TOWN CONNECTOR] Town-based trail network connecting to forest service trails. 5K paved loop through town, 8K to Old Mammoth Rd trails, 15K to Lakes Basin path. Flat to gentle grades. Good for: recovery runs, tempo work, evening runs. Lit sections in town. Safe solo running. Connects to Mammoth Creek trail system.

Extended flat loop through Shady Rest Park and out to the connected Mammoth Lakes Trail System. The park's network of dirt and paved paths meanders through lodgepole pines with connections to the paved Lakes Basin bike path and toward the Village. Dogs on leash welcome.

Extended flat loop through Shady Rest Park and out to the connected Mammoth Lakes Trail System. Network of dirt and paved paths meanders through lodgepole pines with connections to the paved Lakes Basin bike path and the Village. Dogs on leash welcome.

places.name: Sherwin Lakes Trail
places.address: Sherwin Creek Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Small USFS dirt pullout on Sherwin Creek Rd; limited to ~10 cars

activities.notes: 5-mile round trip to a series of small alpine lakes (9,500–9,900 ft) below Sherwin Range. Popular local trail with moderate sustained climb. John Muir Wilderness. Best July–Oct. Bear canisters recommended for overnight.

places.name: Twin Lakes Shoreline Walk
places.address: Twin Lakes Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Tamarack Lodge parking area (163 Twin Lakes Rd); day-use parking available; fee may apply in summer season; restrooms at lodge

activities.notes: Gentle walk along Twin Lakes shoreline through mixed conifer forest at 8,600 ft. Views of Mammoth Crest. Connects to Mammoth Lakes trail system. Can be combined with Tamarack Lodge area paths for longer outing.

places.name: Valentine Lake Trail
places.address: Sherwin Creek Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Sherwin Creek Trailhead parking; free dirt lot off Sherwin Creek Rd; pit toilet; no fee; 4WD helpful on unpaved section

activities.notes: Strenuous 11-mile round trip hike from Sherwin Creek TH to Valentine Lake in the John Muir Wilderness via Inyo NF. Significant elevation gain through open slopes, granite slabs, and meadows. Less crowded than Lakes Basin trails. Wilderness permit required for overnight; good fishing at lake.

places.name: Woolly's 1K Kids Fun Run
places.address: Village at Mammoth, 6201 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
places.parking_notes: Village parking structures

activities.notes: 1 km kids fun run at Mammoth Trail Fest (Saturday morning, 9:30 AM). Open to children 70 inches/feet tall and taller. Village start through trail section. Course map on CalTopo. Part of family festival activities.
activities.conditions_note: Annual July event held during Mammoth Trail Fest weekend

zones.name: Bishop/Eastern Sierra
zones.description: Gateway city to the Eastern Sierra at the foot of the White Mountains, most famous for world-class bouldering at the Buttermilks and Happy Boulders. Also offers premier high-altitude hiking (Bishop Pass, Pine Creek), multi-day JMT access, and year-round trail running.

places.name: Ancient Bristlecone Pine Discovery Trail
places.address: Schulman Grove Visitor Center, White Mountain Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS visitor center lot $5; 23 miles of gravel road; visitor center seasonal

activities.notes: 1-mile interpretive loop at 10,000 ft among 4,000+ year old bristlecone pines (world's oldest trees). ADA accessible boardwalk option. Amazing high desert alpine environment. Start slow at altitude.

places.name: Bishop City Park Loop
places.address: 501 W. China Lake Blvd (Hwy 168 W), Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Free paved parking lot inside Bishop City Park. Large lot, rarely fills. Restrooms and picnic tables on site.

activities.notes: Flat paved loop through Bishop City Park along the Bishop Creek channel. Playground, picnic shelters, skate park, and open lawn adjacent. Stroller and wheelchair accessible. Ideal for toddlers and young children.

places.name: Bishop Creek Canyon Road Run (South Fork)
places.address: South Lake Road / Bishop Creek Canyon, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Start at the lower Bishop Creek Canyon pullout near the LADWP gate area. Free roadside parking. Run on South Lake Road — paved, minimal traffic on weekday mornings.

activities.notes: Out-and-back run on South Lake Road from lower canyon to South Lake (9,755 ft). Passes multiple USFS campgrounds and pullouts. Road winds through Bishop Creek Canyon with views of granite peaks. Excellent training run for high-altitude adaptation.
activities.conditions_note: Summer midday temps in the Owens Valley can exceed 95°F; early morning strongly preferred June–September

places.name: Bishop Creek Pathway
places.address: Bishop Creek Pathway, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: City park lots along pathway; free; paved access

activities.notes: 3-mile paved urban trail along Bishop Creek through town. Multiple access points. Interpretive signs on local history and ecology. Links to parks and shopping. Pleasant even in midday heat.

places.name: Bishop Creek Trail (Lower Canyon Walk)
places.address: West Line Street / Bishop Creek, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Street parking along West Line Street near the creek. No fee. Public access trail along Bishop Creek through the south edge of town.

activities.notes: Riparian walk along Bishop Creek from the south edge of Bishop toward lower canyon. Cottonwood and willow shade; bird watching. Informal but well-worn path. Connects to city trail network.

places.name: Bishop Creek Trail Running Base - South Lake Access
places.address: South Lake Trailhead, North Lake Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS lot $5; bear canisters required overnight; fills weekends

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 10K-42K+ HIGH SIERRA] Gateway to John Muir Trail section. 10K to Chocolate Lakes, 22K to Bishop Pass, 42K+ to Dusy Basin/JMT junction. 7,800-11,972 ft. Epic Sierra high country. Alpine lakes, glaciers, 13,000 ft peaks. Must-do for experienced mountain runners. Acclimatize at lower elevations first. Weather changes fast.
activities.conditions_note: Summer midday temps in the Owens Valley can exceed 95°F; early morning strongly preferred June–September

places.name: Bishop High Country 10K Loop
places.address: South Lake Trailhead, Bishop Creek Canyon, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lot $5; wilderness permit not needed for day use; bear canisters required for overnight

activities.notes: 6.2-mile loop combining Bishop Pass Trail and lake roads. 7,800-9,200 ft elevation. Trail and dirt road mix. Aspen groves, Sierra peaks. Best June-October. Bear country—be aware.
activities.conditions_note: Summer midday temps in the Owens Valley can exceed 95°F; early morning strongly preferred June–September

places.name: Bishop High Sierra Ultra
places.address: Bishop City Park, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: City lot; race day shuttles to remote start points

activities.notes: Annual Bishop High Sierra 50K/100K ultra (September). Routes through pine forests and high desert. 4,000-8,000 ft elevation. Race events; similar training routes available on local trails year-round.
activities.conditions_note: Annual event — verify current dates and registration before planning your trip

places.name: Bishop Pass Trail
places.address: South Lake Trailhead, North Lake Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lot $10; wilderness permit REQUIRED for overnight; bear canisters mandatory

activities.notes: 11-mile round trip to Bishop Pass (11,972 ft). Stunning alpine lakes (Long, Saddlerock, Ruwau). JMT/PCT access. High Sierra peaks. Often snow until July. Thunderstorm risk afternoons.

places.name: Buttermilk Boulders Main Area
places.address: Buttermilk Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: BLM dirt lot; free; fills by 9am weekends; no facilities

activities.notes: World-famous granite bouldering at 5,500 ft. 20+ distinct areas, 1000+ problems. Classics: 'Birthday Direct' (V3), 'Harvey' (V4). Featured in climbing films. Best Oct-May (summer hot). Bring 3+ pads.
activities.conditions_note: Classic Bishop bouldering; summer heat ruins friction — plan for temps below 65°F

places.name: Buttermilk Country Running Trails
places.address: Buttermilk Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: BLM free lot; 4WD helpful but not required; fills with climbers

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-15K HILL INTERVALS] Desert bouldering area service roads and singletrack. 5K rolling warm-up, 10K out-and-back to Sagehen Meadows, 15K loop via old mining roads. 5,200-6,200 ft. Exposed—morning/evening best. Views of Owens Valley, White Mountains. Good for heat training. Less crowded than creek canyon trails. Watch for snakes in summer.
activities.conditions_note: Summer midday temps in the Owens Valley can exceed 95°F; early morning strongly preferred June–September

places.name: Buttermilk Country Trail Run
places.address: Buttermilk Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: BLM free lot at Buttermilk; fills with climbers; no facilities

activities.notes: 5-mile out-and-back on dirt roads and trails through famous bouldering area. 5,200 ft elevation with views of Owens Valley. Connects to old mining roads. Morning best—afternoon heat.
activities.conditions_note: Summer midday temps in the Owens Valley can exceed 95°F; early morning strongly preferred June–September

places.name: Buttermilk Trail Run Course
places.address: Buttermilk Road, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Parking at the Buttermilks main area pull-off on Buttermilk Road, 7 miles west of Bishop. Free BLM land. Dirt lot with space for 30+ vehicles.

activities.notes: Annual trail running event using Buttermilk Road and surrounding BLM terrain west of Bishop. Rolling high-desert terrain with views of the Sierra crest. Multiple distance options typically offered (5K, 10K, half marathon). Also a popular training route year-round. Event organized by local running clubs.
activities.conditions_note: Annual event — verify current dates and registration before planning your trip

places.name: Buttermilks (Buttermilk Country)
places.address: Buttermilk Road, Bishop, CA 93514 (7 miles west of Bishop on Buttermilk Rd)
places.parking_notes: Free BLM open parking on dirt shoulder along Buttermilk Road. Large informal lot at main boulders accommodates 40+ vehicles. No fee. No restrooms on site. Camp dispersed on BLM land nearby (no developed sites). Road is dirt — high clearance recommended in wet weather.

activities.notes: World-class granite bouldering on rounded glacial erratics at 4,700–5,200 ft. Problems range V0–V15. Home to legendary lines including The Mandala (V12), Evilution (V12), and Grandma Peabody (V10). The Druids, Peabody, and High Boulders sectors are the main zones. Best season October–April. Very hot in summer. Magnetic rock; bring more chalk than usual.
activities.conditions_note: Classic Bishop bouldering; summer heat ruins friction — plan for temps below 65°F

places.name: Chocolate Peak (North Slope)
places.address: South Lake Trailhead, Bishop Creek Canyon, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lot $5; wilderness permit area but day use ok without

activities.notes: 7-mile round trip to 11,682 ft. Cross-country after Chocolate Lakes. Class 2 on loose talus. North side only—south face is technical. Views of entire Bishop Creek basin, Hurd Peak, Dusy Basin approach. Good practice with loose rock.
activities.conditions_note: High-elevation routes; afternoon thunderstorms possible — summit before noon

places.name: Happy Boulders
places.address: Happy Boulders, Chalk Bluff Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: BLM lot; $2 donation suggested; 4WD recommended for rough access road

activities.notes: Volcanic tuff bouldering (different rock type than Buttermilks). Higher density of problems, more shade. Grades V0-V10+. Often less crowded than Buttermilks. Good for mid-day sessions. Bring pads.
activities.conditions_note: Best October–April; summer heat degrades volcanic rock friction

places.name: Hurd Peak via South Lake
places.address: South Lake Trailhead, Bishop Creek Canyon, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lot $5; day use; closes for snow

activities.notes: 6-mile round trip off-trail scramble to 12,237 ft summit. Cross-country navigation skills required. Views of entire Bishop Creek basin. Peak bagging destination. Bring map and compass.
activities.conditions_note: High-elevation routes; afternoon thunderstorms possible — summit before noon

places.name: Intake II Lake Walk
places.address: South Lake Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lot $5; day use only; outhouses available

activities.notes: 2-mile lakeside walk on service road. Fishing access, aspen groves. Part of Bishop Creek Canyon system. Good intro to Eastern Sierra at 7,600 ft. Fall colors spectacular in October.

places.name: Kearsarge Pass Trail
places.address: Onion Valley Trailhead, Onion Valley Road, Independence, CA 93526
places.parking_notes: Large USFS paved parking lot at Onion Valley (9,185 ft). Free. Vault toilets. Popular JMT resupply trailhead; lot fills on summer weekends. Wilderness permit required for overnight into Kings Canyon NP. No permit needed for day hike.

activities.notes: Classic Eastern Sierra hike to Kearsarge Pass (11,760 ft) from Onion Valley. Passes Gilbert, Flower, and Heart lakes before the rocky switchbacks to the pass. Views into Kings Canyon NP and the Kearsarge Pinnacles from the top. Trail is well-maintained and heavily used as a JMT entry point. About 45 min south of Bishop via US-395 and Onion Valley Rd.

places.name: Keough's Hot Springs Road Walk
places.address: Keough Hot Springs Road, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Free roadside parking at Keough's Hot Springs entrance. Small private facility; walk is on adjacent BLM open land along the road corridor.

activities.notes: Easy walk on BLM open land in Owens Valley south of Bishop. Views of Sierra Nevada and White Mountains. Desert terrain with sage. Good for young children learning to hike on dirt. No maintained trail; open cross-country on gentle terrain.

places.name: Lake Sabrina Picnic Area Family Walk
places.address: Lake Sabrina Road, Bishop Creek Canyon, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS paved lot at Lake Sabrina. Fee required. Vault toilets. No stroller-friendly path beyond picnic area; carry infants on trail.

activities.notes: Short family-oriented walk from the Lake Sabrina parking area to the picnic sites and boat launch along the lake's east shore. Flat and easy; suitable for ages 3+. Great mountain views and fishing. At 9,128 ft — acclimatize if coming from low elevation.

places.name: Lake Sabrina Shoreline Walk
places.address: Lake Sabrina Road, Bishop Creek Canyon, CA 93514 (end of South Lake Rd fork, Bishop Creek upper canyon)
places.parking_notes: Large paved USFS day-use lot at Lake Sabrina. Fee required (America the Beautiful pass accepted). Fills by mid-morning on weekends in summer. Vault toilets on site.

activities.notes: Paved and maintained gravel path around much of the Lake Sabrina shoreline at 9,128 ft. Outstanding Sierra scenery; fishing access. Mostly flat with minor undulation along the east shore. Family and dog friendly.

places.name: Meysan Lake Trail
places.address: Meysan Lake Trailhead, Lone Pine Canyon, Lone Pine, CA 93545
places.parking_notes: USFS Whitney Portal lot; $10; wilderness permit required for Mt Whitney; fills early

activities.notes: 7.5-mile round trip to Meysan Lake below Mt Whitney. Eastern Sierra dramatic peaks. Less crowded than main Whitney trail. 8,000-11,000 ft elevation. Stunning views of Alabama Hills.

places.name: Mount Tom Trail to False Summit
places.address: North Lake Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Bishop area access via dirt roads; 4WD recommended

activities.notes: 6-mile round trip to 12,600+ ft false summit area on Mt Tom shoulder. Old mining road then trail. Not true summit but high point with excellent views of Owens Valley, White Mountains. Good for acclimatization. Route continues to true summit but requires Class 2+.
activities.conditions_note: High-elevation routes; afternoon thunderstorms possible — summit before noon

places.name: North Lake to Lamarck Col Trailhead Area Walk
places.address: North Lake Road (off Lake Sabrina Road), Bishop Creek Canyon, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Small USFS dirt parking area at North Lake (9,440 ft). Limited to ~20 vehicles. Arrive early (before 7 AM) on summer weekends. Vault toilet. No fee for day use on this spur.

activities.notes: Walk from North Lake parking along the inlet stream and aspen groves toward North Lake shoreline. Aspen color peak in October. Gentle grade; suitable for ages 5+. Out-and-back. Great introduction to the high Sierra for kids. Connects to Piute Pass trailhead.

places.name: Pine Creek Bouldering
places.address: Pine Creek Road, Rovana, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Park at Pine Creek Pack Station lower lot (fee) or informal pullouts along lower Pine Creek Road. Road is paved to pack station; beyond is dirt. Boulders are on Inyo NF land along the creek.

activities.notes: Granite bouldering in lower Pine Creek Canyon on Inyo NF land. Problems range V0–V8. Less developed than Buttermilks or Volcanic Tablelands; fewer crowds and a more exploratory feel. Access via lower Pine Creek Road before the pack station. Best combined with a hike up the canyon.
activities.conditions_note: Bishop bouldering peaks October–April when cooler temps preserve friction; summer heat significantly degrades performance

places.name: Pine Creek Pack Station Walk
places.address: Pine Creek Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS lot at pack station; free; outhouses; seasonal June-Oct

activities.notes: 1.5-mile out-and-back on old mining road. Interpretive signs on 1870s mining. Gentle grade suitable for young children. Views of Owens Valley. Can combine with pack station visit for kids.

places.name: Pine Creek Pass Trail
places.address: Pine Creek Pass Trailhead, Pine Creek Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS lot at pack station; free; high clearance recommended but not required

activities.notes: 8-mile round trip through spectacular canyon to alpine basin at 11,200 ft. Waterfalls in spring. Abandoned tungsten mine ruins. Pass connects to wilderness beyond. Best July-September.

places.name: Piute Pass Trail
places.address: North Lake Trailhead, North Lake Road, Bishop Creek Canyon, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Small USFS dirt lot at North Lake (9,440 ft). ~20 vehicle capacity; arrives full by 7 AM on summer weekends. Vault toilet. Wilderness permit required for overnight (quota applies). Day hike requires no permit.

activities.notes: Piute Pass (11,423 ft) from North Lake TH through John Muir Wilderness. Passes Loch Leven and Piute Lake before the final switchbacks to the pass. Exceptional fall color in aspens below 10,000 ft (September–October). Well-maintained trail. Views of Humphreys Basin from the pass.

places.name: Pleasant Valley / Owens River Gorge Rim Walk
places.address: Pleasant Valley Dam Road, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Free BLM parking at Pleasant Valley Pit Campground area off Pleasant Valley Dam Road, 8.5 miles north of Bishop on US-395.

activities.notes: Flat to gently rolling rim walk above Owens River Gorge on BLM Volcanic Tablelands. Views into the 150-ft basalt gorge. Informal trail on open terrain; stay back from rim edges. No fee.

places.name: Rock Creek Lake Shore Walk
places.address: Rock Creek Lake, Rock Creek Road, Tom's Place, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS day-use parking at Rock Creek Lake (10,850 ft). Small fee. Restrooms available at Rock Creek Lake Resort. Road open typically June–October.

activities.notes: Easy loop around Rock Creek Lake at 10,850 ft. Views of Wheeler Crest and Little Lakes Valley peaks. Popular fishing destination; paved and dirt paths. Accessible for families with young children.

places.name: Rock Creek Lake to Little Lakes Valley (Family Section)
places.address: Rock Creek Lake, Rock Creek Road, Tom's Place, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS day-use lot at Rock Creek Lake. Small fee. Flush toilets at Rock Creek Lake Resort nearby (summer only). Road open June–October.

activities.notes: Gradual walk from Rock Creek Lake TH up into Little Lakes Valley. Passes multiple alpine lakes (Marsh, Heart, Box). Incredible scenery with Mt. Morgan and Bear Creek Spire. Trail is well-maintained and easy to follow. Suitable for ages 6+. Out-and-back to first lake ~2.4 km.

places.name: Rock Creek Road Run
places.address: Rock Creek Road at US-395 (Tom's Place), CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Park at Tom's Place Resort turnoff on US-395 (free pullout). Run up Rock Creek Road — paved, low traffic in early mornings and evenings. No fee.

activities.notes: Out-and-back run up paved Rock Creek Road from Tom's Place (6,998 ft) to Rock Creek Lake (10,850 ft). Stunning canyon scenery. Moderate grade (~4%) suitable for strong recreational runners. Light vehicle traffic. Road closed to cars in winter — excellent snow-free running May through November.
activities.conditions_note: Summer midday temps in the Owens Valley can exceed 95°F; early morning strongly preferred June–September

places.name: Rock Creek to Ruby Lake
places.address: Rock Creek Lake Trailhead, Rock Creek Road, Tom's Place, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS day-use lot at Rock Creek Lake (10,850 ft). Small fee. Vault toilets. Road typically open June–October. No wilderness permit needed for day hike to Ruby Lake.

activities.notes: Out-and-back hike through Little Lakes Valley to Ruby Lake (11,121 ft) in the John Muir Wilderness. Passes Marsh Lake, Heart Lake, Box Lake, Long Lake, and Chickenfoot Lake. One of the most scenic hikes in the Eastern Sierra. Trail is well-maintained. Route continues to Mono Pass for additional mileage.

places.name: Sabrina Lake Shore Walk
places.address: Lake Sabrina, North Lake Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lot $10; closes mid-Nov to late May; boat ramp nearby

activities.notes: 1.2-mile lakeside stroll at 9,128 ft. Dam views, wildflower meadows July-Aug. High altitude—watch for altitude sickness in kids. Fishing pier. Boat rentals available.

places.name: Sad Boulders
places.address: Sad Boulders, Buttermilk Country, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Hike in from Buttermilk parking; 0.5 mile approach; same lot as Buttermilks

activities.notes: Adjacent to Happy Boulders. Similar volcanic tuff. Technical, crimpy problems. V2-V10 range. Good for working technique. Less crowded than main Buttermilk areas. Approach through sagebrush.
activities.conditions_note: Best October–April; summer heat degrades volcanic rock friction

places.name: South Lake Shoreline Walk
places.address: South Lake Road, Bishop Creek Canyon, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS paved lot at South Lake (9,755 ft). Fee required. Restrooms available. Lot fills early on summer weekends; arrive before 8 AM. America the Beautiful pass accepted.

activities.notes: Gentle loop around the west and south shore of South Lake at 9,755 ft elevation. Trout fishing access; stunning views of Mt. Goode and Hurd Peak. Trail is partly on maintained path, partly informal lakeshore. Short and accessible for all fitness levels.

places.name: Volcanic Tablelands Loop Run
places.address: Pleasant Valley Dam Road, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: Free BLM parking at Pleasant Valley Pit area off Pleasant Valley Dam Road. No fee. Vault toilets at campground nearby.

activities.notes: Loop run on BLM dirt roads and open terrain across the Volcanic Tablelands north of Bishop. Flat to gently rolling basalt terrain. Early morning runs offer outstanding views of the Sierra Nevada. Year-round access. Good for base-building before high-altitude runs.
activities.conditions_note: Owens Valley heat makes summer uncomfortable; spring wildflowers late February–April

places.name: Volcanic Tablelands – Fish Slough Sector
places.address: Fish Slough Road, Bishop, CA 93514 (north of Bishop via Five Bridges Rd)
places.parking_notes: Free BLM dispersed parking on Fish Slough Road. No developed facilities. Dirt road accessible to most vehicles in dry conditions. No fee. No restrooms.

activities.notes: Bouldering on the northern end of the Volcanic Tablelands, BLM land north of Bishop. Problems range V0–V7 on featured tuff. Less documented than Happy/Sad drainages; good for beginner to intermediate climbers seeking solitude. Fish Slough is also a wildlife area — be aware of sensitive habitat zones. Year-round access; best October–April.
activities.conditions_note: Owens Valley heat makes summer uncomfortable; spring wildflowers late February–April

places.name: Weir Lake Walk
places.address: South Lake Rd, Bishop, CA 93514
places.parking_notes: USFS fee lot $5; no facilities; closes for snow Nov-May

activities.notes: 1-mile stroll around Weir Lake at 9,000 ft. Small lake with aspen and willow. Good fishing. Quiet alternative to larger South Lake. Often frozen until June.

zones.name: Yosemite Valley
zones.description: Iconic 7-mile glacially carved valley at 3,970 ft in Yosemite National Park, featuring El Capitan, Half Dome, Bridalveil Fall, and Yosemite Falls. World-famous hiking, world-class big wall climbing/bouldering (Camp 4), and family-friendly valley walks. 4M+ annual visitors.

places.name: Bridalveil Fall Trail
places.address: Bridalveil Fall Parking Area, Wawona Road / Southside Drive, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Dedicated parking lot at trailhead (fills quickly in summer). Alternative parking east on Southside Drive with an additional 0.25 mi trail approach. Restrooms in parking area.

activities.notes: Short 0.5 mi (0.8 km) RT paved trail to base of 620-ft Bridalveil Fall, which flows year-round. Western trail section is wheelchair accessible. Leashed pets allowed. Peak flow and spray in spring/early summer; icy in winter. Stay on paved trail — rocks above viewing platform are slippery. ~20 min.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow March–May; substantially reduced by mid-summer

places.name: Bridalveil Fall Walk
places.address: Bridalveil Fall Trailhead, Southside Dr, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Large paved lot; fills by 9am; bike or shuttle recommended

activities.notes: 1.5-mile round trip paved trail to 620-ft waterfall base. Wet and slippery from spray year-round. Wind can push spray dramatically. Beautiful rainbows in afternoon. Accessible but steep final section.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow March–May; substantially reduced by mid-summer

places.name: Camp 4 Bouldering
places.address: Camp 4, Southside Dr, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Camp 4 day-use parking (lottery required); or park at Yosemite Valley Lodge and walk

activities.notes: World-famous granite bouldering birthplace. Classics: 'Midnight Lightning' (V8), 'Cocaine Corner' (V2). Free camping area. Historic climbing culture. Bring multiple crash pads. Best spring/fall—summer hot.
activities.conditions_note: Avoid wet or damp rock; Camp 4 area accessible year-round when dry

places.name: Camp 4 Bouldering Area
places.address: Camp 4, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Camp 4 parking is for registered campers only. Public parking at Yosemite Falls parking area directly across from Camp 4 entrance on Northside Drive. Free shuttle to Stop #7.

activities.notes: Camp 4 is listed on the National Register of Historic Places for its seminal role in the development of rock climbing as a sport. The boulders immediately around Camp 4 are world-famous, featuring problems ranging V0 to V14 on Yosemite granite. Columbia Boulder and the Camp 4 boulders are the primary attractions. Follow established approach trails. Minimize chalk use; clean holds after use per NPS bouldering regulations. Consider environmental impact before deploying crash pads. No permit required for day-use bouldering.
activities.conditions_note: Avoid wet or damp rock; Camp 4 area accessible year-round when dry

places.name: Church Bowl Bouldering
places.address: Church Bowl, Southside Dr near Yosemite Chapel, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Yosemite Chapel lot or street parking; walk uphill to boulders

activities.notes: Historic bouldering area with easier problems. Grades V0-V4. Great for beginners to intermediate. Better shade than Camp 4 in summer. Mix of slab and vertical granite.
activities.conditions_note: Avoid wet or damp rock; Camp 4 area accessible year-round when dry

places.name: Columbia Boulder
places.address: Near Camp 4, Northside Drive, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Park at Yosemite Falls parking area on Northside Drive, a short walk from Camp 4. Free shuttle to Stop #7.

activities.notes: Columbia Boulder is one of the most historically significant boulders in Yosemite and American climbing history. Located immediately adjacent to Camp 4. Features classic problems on high-quality Yosemite granite. Wide range of difficulty. NPS regulations require following established approach trails, minimizing chalk, and cleaning holds after use. Crash pad use should consider impact on fragile vegetation.
activities.conditions_note: Avoid wet or damp rock; Camp 4 area accessible year-round when dry

places.name: Cook's Meadow Loop
places.address: Yosemite Valley Visitor Center, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Valley Visitor Center lot; free shuttle; bike path access

activities.notes: 1-mile flat boardwalk through meadow with views of Half Dome, Yosemite Falls, El Capitan. Best photography at golden hour. Wheelchair accessible. Interpretive signs about valley ecology.

places.name: Cook's Meadow Loop (Family Walk)
places.address: Yosemite Valley Visitor Center, Yosemite Village, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at trailhead. Use Yosemite Village parking area or Yosemite Falls parking area. Shuttle Stop #5 or #6.

activities.notes: Completely flat paved loop perfect for young children, strollers, and wheelchairs (when not snow-covered). Views of Yosemite Falls, Half Dome, Glacier Point, and Sentinel Rock. Bicycles and leashed pets allowed. Boardwalks can be slippery when wet. Restrooms and water at trailhead. ~30 min.

places.name: Four Mile Trail
places.address: Four Mile Trailhead, Southside Drive, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Limited parking along Southside Drive at trailhead near Sentinel Rock. Accessible via Valleywide Shuttle Stop #11. Parking also at Glacier Point (trail's upper terminus).

activities.notes: 4.8 mi (7.7 km) one-way; 9.6 mi (15.5 km) RT. 3,200 ft (975 m) gain to Glacier Point. Continuous steep grade following historic 1872 toll trail route. Views of El Capitan, Yosemite Falls, Half Dome, and Sierra peaks. Union Point viewpoint ~2/3 up trail. Many drop-offs and ledges — not for inexperienced hikers. No water on trail; carry 2 qts one-way. Opens ~May; closes below Union Point after significant snow (Nov–Dec).

places.name: Four Mile Trail Run
places.address: Four Mile Trailhead, Southside Dr near Sentinel Beach, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Sentinel Beach picnic area lot; fills early; winter closure upper sections

activities.notes: 6-mile round trip to Glacier Point. 3,200 ft gain—one of the biggest in the valley. Singletrack switchbacks with epic views. Run up, shuttle down or reverse. Best April-November.

places.name: Glacier Point Road Trail Running Access
places.address: Glacier Point Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Multiple pullouts along road; seasonal May-Nov; $35 park entrance

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 10K-30K HIGH ELEVATION] 7,000-7,500 ft running along paved road (closed to cars in AM before 9am or after 4pm). Connects Sentinel Dome, Taft Point, McGurk Meadow, Bridalveil Creek trails. 10K to Sentinel Dome and back, 16K to Ostrander Lake trailhead, 30K+ point-to-point to Wawona. High Sierra meadows, sequoia groves. Less crowded than valley.
activities.conditions_note: Road typically open May–November; check nps.gov/yose for current status

places.name: Glacier Point Viewpoint Walk
places.parking_notes: Glacier Point parking area at end of Glacier Point Road (32 mi from Yosemite Valley via Wawona Rd). Road closes Nov–May. Parking fills by late morning — arrive before 9am or take the Glacier Point Tour Bus from the Valley.

activities.notes: Short paved walk from Glacier Point parking to the famous viewpoint at 7,214 ft. Panoramic view of Half Dome, Vernal and Nevada Falls, and the Valley floor 3,200 ft below. Railings and paved path make it accessible for all ages. The single most dramatic viewpoint in Yosemite — arguably worth the drive even with kids.

Walk around the Glacier Point rim area — main overlook, Overhanging Rock (historic), Geology Hut, and Ahwahnee Amphitheater point. All paved or packed surface. Absorbing views in every direction. Can extend by descending the Four Mile Trail toward the Valley floor (strenuous, 4.8 mi one-way).

places.name: Half Dome Trail
places.address: Happy Isles Trailhead, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Yosemite Valley shuttle stop #16; advance wilderness permit REQUIRED

activities.notes: 17-mile round trip via Mist Trail or JMT. 4,800 ft total gain. Iconic 400-ft cable section to 8,839 ft summit. Permit via lottery ($10 + $10/person). Start before 5am. Seasonal cables May-October.

places.name: Happy Isles Nature Center Area
places.address: Happy Isles, Shuttle Stop 16, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at Happy Isles. Nearest parking at Curry Village (~0.75 mi). Use free Valleywide or East Valley Shuttle to Stop #16.

activities.notes: Flat walk from the Happy Isles shuttle stop to the nature center footbridges and river junction. The Merced River rushing through the rocky Happy Isles channels is a highlight for kids. Nature center (when open) has exhibits on Yosemite ecology. Flat, accessible, ADA-friendly section.

Flat walk to the Happy Isles footbridges and Merced River junction. The river rushing through the rocky channels is a highlight for kids. Nature center has ecology exhibits. Flat, ADA-accessible section. A natural gathering point at the east end of the Valley.

Easy stroll around Happy Isles nature area on the valley floor along the Merced River. The John Muir Trail and Mist Trail both start here. Good for wildlife viewing and nature interpretation. The Happy Isles Art and Nature Center is seasonal.

places.name: John Muir Trail Segment in Yosemite Valley
places.address: Happy Isles Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 16, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at Happy Isles. Park at Curry Village (~0.75 mi). Free East Valley Shuttle to Stop #16.

activities.notes: JMT segment from Happy Isles to top of Nevada Fall (5.4 mi / 8.7 km one-way via Nevada Fall route). Alternative to the steeper Mist Trail. Rocky trail run with significant elevation gain. Passes Vernal Fall (317 ft) and Nevada Fall (594 ft). No pets or strollers. Water at shuttle stop #16 and footbridge (May–Oct only). Carry 3–4 quarts.

places.name: Lower Yosemite Fall Trail
places.address: Lower Yosemite Fall Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 6, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at the trailhead. Use Yosemite Village or Yosemite Falls parking areas. Free Valleywide Visitor Shuttle to Stop #6 (7 am–10 pm). Can walk from Valley Visitor Center (~0.5 mi extra).

activities.notes: NPS most-visited trail in Yosemite. 1 mi (1.6 km) loop to base of 320-ft Lower Yosemite Fall. Eastern portion is wheelchair accessible. Strollers permitted. Drinking fountain and restrooms along trail. Rocks are slippery even when dry — stay on path. Open year-round; often dry late July–October. ~30 min.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May from snowmelt; upper falls may cease by August

places.name: Mirror Lake Flat Loop (Paved Section)
places.address: Mirror Lake Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 17, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at trailhead. Park at Curry Village or anywhere in eastern Yosemite Valley and take free shuttle to Stop #17. Bicycles allowed on the first paved mile.

activities.notes: Paved out-and-back service road to Mirror Lake (2 mi / 3.2 km RT). Views of Half Dome reflections (best spring/early summer), Tenaya Canyon, Washington Column. Stroller and wheelchair accessible with assistance on paved section. Lake becomes a meadow in late summer. ~1 hour.

places.name: Mirror Lake Loop
places.address: Mirror Lake Trailhead, Happy Isles Loop Rd, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Yosemite Valley shuttle stop #17; seasonal road closure winter; fills summer

activities.notes: 2.5-mile paved and dirt loop to seasonal Mirror Lake. Best in spring/early summer when full. Reflections of Half Dome. Sandy beach areas. ADA accessible to first viewpoint.

places.name: Mirror Lake Loop (Family Walk - Flat Paved Section)
places.address: Mirror Lake Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 17, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at trailhead. Park at Curry Village or use free shuttle to Stop #17. Bicycles allowed on paved first mile.

activities.notes: Full Mirror Lake loop (5 mi / 8 km, 2-3 hrs) or easy 2 mi RT to the lake on paved road. Paved first mile is stroller and wheelchair accessible (with assistance). Views of Half Dome. Ecology and cultural history exhibits. No water on trail — bring your own. Vault toilets at 0.5 mi and 1 mi. Lake may be dry meadow in late summer.

places.name: Mist Trail Run to Top of Vernal Fall
places.address: Happy Isles Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 16, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at Happy Isles. Park at Curry Village (~0.75 mi). Free Valleywide or East Valley Shuttle to Stop #16.

activities.notes: Trail run up Mist Trail from Happy Isles to top of Vernal Fall (2.4 mi / 3.9 km RT). Over 600 granite steps. Significant spray in spring/early summer — trail is slick. Common for sprained ankles and knee injuries. No pets, bicycles, or strollers. Carry 2 quarts water.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May; expect heavy mist — waterproof layers recommended

places.name: Mist Trail to Nevada Fall
places.address: Happy Isles Trailhead, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Yosemite Valley shuttle stop #16; wilderness permit required for beyond Nevada Fall

activities.notes: 6.5-mile round trip past Vernal to 594-ft Nevada Fall. Continue past John Muir Trail junction. 'Giant Staircase' of granite steps. Dry sections by late summer. Combine with JMT for loop.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May; expect heavy mist — waterproof layers recommended

places.name: Mist Trail to Vernal Fall
places.address: Happy Isles Trailhead, Happy Isles Loop Rd, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Yosemite Valley shuttle stop #16; no private vehicles; early arrival essential

activities.notes: 3.2-mile round trip to 317-ft Vernal Fall. 'Mist' from waterfall drenches hikers—bring rain gear. Granite steps (600+ of them). Spectacular in spring runoff. Slippery when wet.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May; expect heavy mist — waterproof layers recommended

places.name: Mist Trail — Vernal Fall & Nevada Fall
places.address: Happy Isles Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 16, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at Happy Isles. Park at Curry Village (~0.75 mi). Free Valleywide or East Valley Shuttle to Stop #16. Shuttle starts 7 AM.

activities.notes: Top of Vernal Fall via Mist Trail: 2.4 mi (3.9 km) RT, 1,000 ft (300 m) gain. Over 600 granite steps. Significant mist/spray in spring. Strenuous. 3 hrs. Carry 2 quarts water. No pets, strollers, or bikes.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May; expect heavy mist — waterproof layers recommended

activities.notes: Top of Nevada Fall via Mist Trail: 5.4 mi (8.7 km) RT, 2,000 ft (610 m) gain. Nevada Fall drops 594 ft. Strenuous. 5–6 hrs. Carry 3–4 quarts water. JMT offers alternate longer route (~4 mi one-way). Swimming illegal at Emerald Pool and Silver Apron.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May; expect heavy mist — waterproof layers recommended

places.name: North Dome via Yosemite Falls Trail
places.address: Camp 4 or Yosemite Lodge, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Camp 4 day use (lottery) or park at Yosemite Valley Lodge

activities.notes: 8.7-mile round trip to 7,542 ft dome with 360° views of Half Dome, Valley, Yosemite Falls. Trail to Yosemite Falls, then traverse to North Dome. Mostly walking with brief Class 2 section. One of best valley viewpoints without technical climbing. Return via Indian Rock or same route.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May from snowmelt; upper falls may cease by August

places.name: Sentinel Dome (Walk-up Route)
places.address: Sentinel Dome Trailhead, Glacier Point Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Glacier Point Rd lot; $35 park entrance; road seasonal May-Oct

activities.notes: 2-mile round trip to 8,122 ft granite dome summit. Paved to dirt trail then granite walk-up—no scrambling required. Arguably best easy summit in Yosemite: views of Half Dome, El Capitan, Yosemite Falls, entire valley. Accessible to most fitness levels. Sunset spectacular.
activities.conditions_note: Glacier Point Road seasonal — typically May through November

places.name: Sentinel Dome Trail
places.address: Sentinel Dome / Taft Point Trailhead, Glacier Point Road, Yosemite, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Dedicated parking lot at Sentinel Dome / Taft Point Trailhead on Glacier Point Road. Road open late May through November (weather permitting). Drive from Yosemite Valley via Wawona Road and Glacier Point Road.

activities.notes: 2.3 mi (3.7 km) RT from Glacier Point Road trailhead to summit of Sentinel Dome (8,122 ft). Moderate. 360° panoramic views of Yosemite Valley, Half Dome, El Capitan, Yosemite Falls. Final 0.3 mi is a scramble on bare granite dome. No shade; bring sun protection. Can be combined with Taft Point for a 5 mi loop. Glacier Point Road typically open late May–November.
activities.conditions_note: Glacier Point Road seasonal — typically May through November

places.name: Snow Creek Trail
places.address: Mirror Lake Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 17, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at Mirror Lake Trailhead. Park at Curry Village or anywhere in eastern valley and take free shuttle to Stop #17.

activities.notes: 9.4 mi (15.1 km) RT from valley floor to valley rim via dozens of switchbacks. 2,700 ft (820 m) gain. Views of Half Dome, Quarter Domes, Clouds Rest. Less crowded than other rim hikes. Exposed with little shade; rattlesnakes present. Loose sand and rocky terrain — ankle injuries common. 6–7 hrs. No water on trail. Popular winter snowshoe/ski route.

places.name: Swinging Bridge
places.address: Swinging Bridge, Southside Dr, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Small pullout on Southside Drive; bike recommended; 0.1 mile walk from road

activities.notes: 0.2-mile walk to pedestrian suspension bridge over Merced River. Views of Yosemite Falls. Sandy beach below bridge for wading. Quiet alternative to crowded spots. Excellent for toddlers.

places.name: Swinging Bridge / Sentinel Beach Area Walk
places.address: Southside Drive, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Limited roadside parking along Southside Drive near Sentinel Beach picnic area. Valley Loop Trail accessible from shuttle stops #11 and #12.

activities.notes: Easy out-and-back or connector segment of the Valley Loop Trail along the Merced River past Swinging Bridge and Sentinel Beach. Mostly level dirt and old pavement. Good views of El Capitan and Cathedral Rocks.

places.name: Taft Point to The Fissures
places.address: Taft Point Trailhead, Glacier Point Rd, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Glacier Point Rd pullout; $35 park entrance; seasonal road

activities.notes: 2-mile easy walk to 7,503 ft viewpoint—more viewpoint than peak but included for perspective. Dramatic vertical cliff edge (fissures) with valley views. Flat trail, minimal elevation. Good for building comfort with heights. Keep children back from cliff edge—serious exposure here.
activities.conditions_note: Glacier Point Road seasonal; serious unguarded cliff exposure — keep children close

places.name: Upper Yosemite Falls Trail
places.address: Yosemite Falls Trailhead, near Camp 4, Shuttle Stop 7, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Camp 4 parking is for campers only. Public parking at Yosemite Falls parking area across the street from and just beyond Camp 4 entrance. Free shuttle to Stop #7.

activities.notes: 7.2 mi (11.6 km) RT to top of Yosemite Falls (2,425 ft / 739 m above valley floor). Historic trail built 1873–1877. Strenuous switchback ascent through oak woodland. 6–8 hrs RT. Views of Half Dome, Sentinel Rock, Yosemite Valley. No water on trail — carry 4 quarts. Steep upper section with exposure and loose footing. Can extend to Yosemite Point or Eagle Peak.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May from snowmelt; upper falls may cease by August

places.name: Valley Loop Trail (Run)
places.address: Lower Yosemite Fall Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 6, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No dedicated trailhead parking. Use Yosemite Village or Yosemite Falls parking areas. Multiple shuttle access points at stops #6, #9, #11 along the route.

activities.notes: Full 11.5 mi (18.5 km) loop run around Yosemite Valley on mixed terrain: dirt, rocks, sand, old pavement. Mostly level with some hilly sections. Passes El Capitan, Bridalveil Fall, Swinging Bridge, Sentinel Beach, and Valley views. Pets, bicycles, and strollers prohibited on trail. No water along route — carry your own. 5–7 hr hike; faster for runners.
Half Valley Loop run (7.2 mi / 11.6 km). Good morning trail run option on the less-crowded western valley section. Mixed terrain. 2.5–3.5 hr hiking pace.

places.name: Valley View / El Capitan Meadow Walk
places.address: El Capitan Meadow, Northside Drive, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Roadside pullouts along Northside Drive at El Capitan Meadow. Shuttle stop #9 (Valley View area). Yosemite Falls parking area also accessible.

activities.notes: Easy flat walk through El Capitan Meadow with full-face views of El Capitan (3,000 ft wall). Paved path along the Valley floor. Watch climbers on the wall with binoculars. Picnic tables nearby. One of the best family photo spots in the Valley.

Easy flat walk through El Capitan Meadow with full-face views of El Capitan (3,000 ft wall). Paved path along the Valley floor. Watch climbers with binoculars. Picnic tables nearby. One of the best family photo spots in the Valley.

Flat walk along Valley Loop Trail through El Capitan Meadow with unobstructed views of El Capitan (3,000 ft granite face). Excellent spot to watch climbers on El Cap with binoculars. Mostly level trail on dirt/old pavement. Leashed pets prohibited on Valley Loop Trail.

places.name: Valley View Loop
places.address: Valley View, Northside Dr, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Small pullout on Northside Drive; can fill with photographers at sunset

activities.notes: Short riverside walk at iconic view of El Capitan, Bridalveil Fall, and Cathedral Rocks. One of Ansel Adams' famous photo locations. Picnic spots on sandbars. Gentle grade for all ages.

places.name: Vernal Fall Footbridge (Family Hike)
places.address: Happy Isles Trailhead, Shuttle Stop 16, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: No parking at Happy Isles. Park at Curry Village (~0.75 mi away). Free Valleywide or East Valley Shuttle to Stop #16. Shuttle starts at 7 AM.

activities.notes: 1.6 mi (2.6 km) RT to Vernal Fall Footbridge via paved trail from Happy Isles (Mist Trail). First paved mile is the busiest trail segment in Yosemite. Views of 317-ft Vernal Fall. Water available at shuttle stop #16 and footbridge (May–Oct). Pets, bicycles, and strollers prohibited beyond Happy Isles. ~1–1.5 hrs.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May; mist can be heavy spring — waterproof layers

places.name: Wawona Meadow Loop - Historic Running
places.address: Wawona Hotel, Wawona, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Hotel lot for guests or day use fee; free along Wawona Rd

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-12K MELLOW] Historic 4-mile loop around Wawona Meadow. Gentle, rolling, forested. Historic hotel views. 5K out-and-back on meadow edge, 6.4K full loop, 12K combined with Swinging Bridge. 4,000 ft elevation. Accessible year-round (snow possible Dec-Mar). Quiet alternative to valley crowds. Good for recovery runs.

places.name: Yosemite Falls Lower Walk
places.address: Lower Yosemite Fall Trailhead, Northside Dr, Yosemite Village, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Yosemite Valley free shuttle stop #6; day-use lot fills by 10am; bike recommended

activities.notes: 1.2-mile paved loop to base of Lower Yosemite Fall (320 ft). ADA accessible. Views of both Upper (1,430 ft) and Lower falls. Spray can be intense in spring. Open year-round.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May from snowmelt; upper falls may cease by August

places.name: Yosemite Falls Trail
places.address: Camp 4 or Yosemite Lodge area, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Camp 4 walk-in (lottery required); or park at Yosemite Valley Lodge

activities.notes: 7.2-mile round trip to top of 2,425-ft Yosemite Falls (North America's tallest). Extremely steep switchbacks. Columbia Rock viewpoint at 1 mile. Seasonal—waterfall dry late summer. Avoid ice in winter.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow April–May from snowmelt; upper falls may cease by August

places.name: Yosemite Half Marathon
places.address: Yosemite Valley, start near Yosemite Lodge, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Event day shuttle from remote lots; reservation required for race

activities.notes: Annual Yosemite Half Marathon (May). Paved and dirt roads through valley with views of all major formations. 4,000 ft elevation. Self-guided training routes available year-round on similar terrain.
activities.conditions_note: Annual August event — verify dates and entry at recreation.gov

places.name: Yosemite Valley Bouldering Circuit (Camp 4 Area)
places.address: Camp 4 / Northside Drive, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Yosemite Falls parking area on Northside Drive. Camp 4 parking for campers only. Free shuttle Stop #7.

activities.notes: Beginner-friendly circuit of V0–V3 problems on boulders in and around Camp 4. Good introductory Yosemite granite bouldering. Some of the best bouldering in Yosemite per NPS climbing page. NPS regulations: do not remove lichen or vegetation to establish problems; remove all trash including tape wrappers; minimize chalk; use established approach trails.
activities.conditions_note: Avoid wet or damp rock; Camp 4 area accessible year-round when dry

activities.notes: Advanced circuit targeting V8–V14 problems across Camp 4 and Columbia Boulder area. Elite granite bouldering with historic problems. Sessions may span multiple days. Follow NPS Leave No Trace bouldering guidelines. Carry out all waste. Assess environmental impact before placing crash pads on fragile vegetation.
activities.conditions_note: Avoid wet or damp rock; Camp 4 area accessible year-round when dry

places.name: Yosemite Valley Bouldering — Lower Valley Area
places.address: Southside Drive, Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Roadside parking along Southside Drive. Accessible via Shuttle Stop #11 or #12.

activities.notes: Scattered bouldering areas in the lower/western Yosemite Valley, away from Camp 4. Less crowded than Camp 4 circuits. Yosemite Valley offers some of the best bouldering in the park per NPS. Access via established trails.
