Per NPS climbing regulations: minimize chalk, clean holds when finished, do not alter rock or vegetation to establish problems, consider crash pad impact on vegetation.
activities.conditions_note: Avoid wet or damp rock; Camp 4 area accessible year-round when dry

places.name: Yosemite Valley Loop Trail
places.address: Yosemite Valley, multiple access points, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Start at any valley location; bike to create loop; free shuttle between points

activities.notes: The eastern Valley Loop section — from Curry Village/Upper Pines along the Valley floor past Happy Isles to Swinging Bridge and back — is a perfect flat family walk on paved/packed trail. Exceptional El Cap and Half Dome views. Deer are commonly seen in meadows morning and evening.

Eastern Valley Loop from Curry Village/Upper Pines along the Valley floor past Happy Isles to Swinging Bridge and back — a perfect flat family walk on paved trail. Exceptional El Cap and Half Dome views. Deer are commonly seen in meadows at dawn and dusk.
7-mile loop on paved bike path and dirt trails around valley floor. Mix of forest, meadow, and river. Elevation 4,000 ft. Good training run with iconic views throughout. Best at sunrise.

Full 7-mile flat loop around Yosemite Valley floor connecting all major landmarks: El Capitan Meadow, Valley View, Sentinel Beach, Swinging Bridge, Cook's Meadow, Happy Isles, Mirror Lake connector. Paved and packed trail throughout. Best done early morning for quiet and wildlife. Can be done in sections — most people walk 2–4 miles of it.

Full 7-mile flat loop around Yosemite Valley floor connecting all major landmarks: El Capitan Meadow, Valley View, Sentinel Beach, Swinging Bridge, Cook's Meadow, Happy Isles, Mirror Lake connector. Paved and packed trail throughout. Best done early morning for quiet and wildlife. Most people walk 2–4 miles of it.

places.name: Yosemite Valley Loop Trail Network
places.address: Yosemite Village, multiple access points, Yosemite National Park, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Day-use lots fill early; use free shuttle; bike/run in from Curry Village

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-21K VALLEY FLOOR] Paved bike path and dirt trail combo around valley floor. 5K east valley, 10K full loop, 21K combined with Mirror Lake extension. 4,000 ft elevation. Flat to gentle grades. Iconic views throughout. Start 5am to avoid crowds. Run beneath El Cap, past Yosemite Falls. Bucket list even for non-runners. Crowded mid-day.

places.name: Yosemite Valley Marathon / Half Marathon (Event)
places.address: Yosemite Valley, CA 95389
places.parking_notes: Event-specific parking and shuttle arrangements apply during race. Check race organizer website for details.

activities.notes: Organized marathon event using Yosemite Valley roads and trails. Route utilizes Valley Loop Trail and Valley roads. Permit-based event run on mostly flat-to-rolling terrain through the valley floor. Check NPS special use permit events calendar for scheduled dates.
activities.conditions_note: Annual event — verify current dates and registration before planning your trip

activities.notes: Half marathon distance option. Valley floor route on roads and Valley Loop Trail. Flat to gently rolling. Event-based only.
activities.conditions_note: Annual event — verify current dates and registration before planning your trip

zones.name: Sequoia/Kings Canyon
zones.description: Twin national parks encompassing the Giant Forest (world's largest trees by volume), Kings Canyon (North America's deepest canyon), Cedar Grove, and General Grant Grove. Strong hiking, walking, and family recreation. Managed jointly by NPS but distinct in character.

places.name: Alta Peak via Alta Trail
places.address: Wolverton Trailhead, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Wolverton Parking Area off Wolverton Rd, between Lodgepole and Giant Forest. Large paved lot with restrooms. One of the best-maintained lots in the park.

activities.notes: Strenuous 14-mile round trip from Wolverton (6,800 ft) to Alta Peak summit (11,204 ft). Gains ~4,800 ft total. Long day hike through forests, meadows, and exposed granite. Panoramic 360° views including Mt. Whitney on clear days. Start early; bring layers. Snow possible into July. One of Sequoia's premier summit hikes.

places.name: Auto Log Walk
places.address: Auto Log, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Moro Rock parking area; shuttle stop; free with park entrance

activities.notes: Short walk to fallen sequoia once used as auto tunnel (now too decayed to drive on). Interpretive signs. Historic 1920s tourist attraction. Combine with Moro Rock climb.

places.name: Big Baldy Ridge Trail Run
places.address: Big Baldy Trailhead, Generals Hwy, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Small pullout / trailhead parking on Generals Highway, 8 miles south of Grant Grove Village. No facilities. Limited spaces.

activities.notes: 4.4-mile round-trip run to Big Baldy summit (8,209 ft) with 600-ft gain. Well-defined trail on granite ridge with views of Redwood Canyon, Buck Rock Fire Tower, and Sierra. Good moderate trail run with non-technical terrain.

places.name: Big Stump Loop (Kings Canyon Entrance)
places.address: Big Stump Entrance, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Small parking area just past Big Stump Entrance Station on Hwy 180. Free with park pass. Accessible lot.

activities.notes: 2-mile loop through remnants of a late-1800s logged giant sequoia grove. Massive stumps and second-growth trees; interpretive history of logging era. Meadow for wildlife viewing. Good intro hike for kids, with interesting historical context.

places.name: Big Trees Trail
places.address: Giant Forest Museum, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Giant Forest Museum lot; shuttle access; free with park entrance

activities.notes: 1.3-mile paved loop on meadow edge with views of massive sequoias. ADA accessible. Audio tour available. Ranger programs at museum. Sunset views of sequoias against meadow.
activities.conditions_note: Round Meadow boardwalk; accessible most of year

places.name: Big Trees Trail (Round Meadow Loop)
places.address: Giant Forest Museum, 47050 Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Accessible parking directly at Giant Forest Museum trailhead. Large paved lot with accessible spaces. Free summer shuttle from Lodgepole/Wuksachi.

activities.notes: Paved 0.75-mile accessible loop circling Round Meadow near Giant Forest Museum. Trailside panels describe giant sequoia ecology. Flat, wheelchair accessible, ~1 hour round trip. One of the best introductory sequoia experiences.
activities.conditions_note: Round Meadow boardwalk; accessible most of year

places.name: Bubbs Creek Wall — Charlotte Dome Bouldering Approach
places.address: Road's End Trailhead, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Road's End parking at end of Hwy 180 in Cedar Grove. Large gravel lot. Backcountry permit required for overnight trips. Day access for the first few miles is permit-free.

activities.notes: NPS identifies the Bubbs Creek area (8-mile hike from Road's End) as offering multi-pitch crack climbing near Charlito Dome and Charlotte Dome. Remote granite bouldering and crack climbing at elevation. Best for experienced climbers. No motorized equipment or permanent fixed anchors without permit. Access road open May–November.

places.name: Buena Vista Peak (via old fire road)
places.address: Buena Vista Peak Rd, Giant Forest, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Giant Forest area access; seasonal road closure; $35 park entrance

activities.notes: 4-mile round trip to 7,600+ ft summit via old fire road then short cross-country. Views of western divide, Central Valley. Often overlooked peak in Giant Forest area. Class 1 walking. Good for practicing map reading—trail not always obvious.
activities.conditions_note: High elevation; afternoon thunderstorms possible — off summit by 1pm

places.name: Buena Vista Peak Bouldering
places.address: Buena Vista Peak Trailhead, Generals Hwy, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Small trailhead parking area just south of Kings Canyon Overlook on Generals Highway, 6 miles southeast of Grant Grove Village. A few cars; roadside pullout. No facilities.

activities.notes: NPS lists Buena Vista Peak (8,209 ft, 2-mile round trip) as one of the easiest rock climbing/bouldering approaches in the parks. Granite dome with scattered boulders and low-angle slabs. 360° views of sequoias in Redwood Canyon and Sierra Nevada. Best for beginner-to-intermediate bouldering. No technical gear required for basic scrambling.

places.name: Cedar Grove Overlook via Hotel Creek Trail
places.address: Cedar Grove Visitor Center, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Park at Cedar Grove Visitor Center / Village area on Hwy 180. Trail crosses highway from visitor center. Passes through 2024 Happy Fire burn area — check current conditions for downed trees and trail damage.

activities.notes: 5-mile round trip gaining 1,200 ft via Hotel Creek Trail on south-facing slope. Switchbacks up through chaparral to forested ridge and rocky outcrop with gorgeous panoramic view up and down Kings Canyon. Allow 3-4 hours. Best done in cooler morning hours. Goes through 2024 Happy Fire burn area.

places.name: Cedar Grove River Run
places.address: Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Visitor center lot; free; seasonal closure winter

activities.notes: 5-mile out-and-back along South Fork Kings River. Canyon floor at 4,600 ft. Flat to gentle grade. Sandy beaches for cool-off. Views of canyon walls. Shaded sections.

places.name: Cedar Grove River Trail Network
places.address: Cedar Grove Visitor Center, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Visitor center lot; free; seasonal April-Nov; road closes with snow

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-25K CANYON RUNNING] 4,600 ft canyon floor running along South Fork Kings River. 5K Zumwalt Meadow loop, 10K Roads End trail, 16K Mist Falls, 25K+ to Paradise Valley. Canyon walls 4,000 ft overhead. River crossings (easy). Dramatic scenery unique to this zone. Hot midday—early morning best. Limited shade—bring water.

places.name: Cedar Grove River Walk
places.address: Cedar Grove Visitor Center, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: NPS visitor center lot; free; seasonal—closes with first heavy snow

activities.notes: 1.5-mile riverside stroll along South Fork Kings River. Canyon floor at 4,600 ft. Sandy beaches for wading. Views of Grand Sentinel and North Dome. Best April-November.

places.name: Circle Meadow Loop
places.address: Giant Forest area, Crescent Meadow Rd, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Crescent Meadow lot; shuttle access; seasonal road closure winter

activities.notes: 1.5-mile loop through sequoia forest to Circle Meadow. Views of Moro Rock from forest. Log bridges, wildflowers. Quiet alternative to Sherman Tree crowds. Giant sequoias throughout.

places.name: Congress Trail Loop
places.address: Wolverton Road, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Congress Trail / Sherman Tree Parking Lot off Wolverton Rd. Accessible parking available 2 miles north on Generals Highway near Giant Forest Museum. Summer free shuttle from Lodgepole/Wuksachi.

activities.notes: NPS most-visited trail. Paved 2-mile loop beginning near General Sherman Tree. Passes dozens of named giant sequoias including President Tree, Chief Sequoyah, and Senate/House groups. Some gentle grades and stairs. World's largest trees by volume.
activities.conditions_note: Accessible year-round; may have snow patches December–March

places.name: General Grant Tree Trail
places.address: General Grant Tree Trailhead, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: General Grant Tree Parking Area, 1 mile northwest of Kings Canyon Visitor Center on Hwy 180. Large paved lot, accessible spaces. No shuttle in Grant Grove currently.

activities.notes: Short paved 0.33-mile loop to General Grant Tree — the Nation's Christmas Tree (proclaimed by President Coolidge, 1926) and world's third-largest tree by volume. Also passes Gamlin Cabin and the Fallen Monarch, a massive hollow log.

places.name: General Sherman Tree Accessible Path
places.address: Wolverton Rd Trailhead, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Accessible parking at Wolverton Rd lot (near Sherman Tree). Alternatively, accessible parking on Generals Highway 2 miles north of Giant Forest Museum; paved accessible trail leads to tree base. Summer free shuttle stops at Sherman Tree.

activities.notes: 0.5-mile paved path descending from Wolverton Rd to the world's largest tree by volume (General Sherman Tree, 274.9 ft tall, 36.5 ft diameter at base). Path includes some stairs; accessible alternative from Generals Hwy is flat and paved. Return is uphill. Strollers manageable on accessible route.
activities.conditions_note: Accessible year-round from Lodgepole; trail may be snowy December–March

places.name: General Sherman Tree Walk
places.address: General Sherman Tree Trail, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: NPS paved lot; fills by 9am; shuttle available from Lodgepole; $35 park entry fee

activities.notes: 0.6-mile paved loop to world's largest tree by volume (52,500 cu ft). Giant Forest wheelchair accessible. Fallen monarch tunnel. Information plaques. Most visited site in park.
activities.conditions_note: Accessible year-round from Lodgepole; trail may be snowy December–March

places.name: Generals Highway Road Run — Ash Mountain to Giant Forest
places.address: Ash Mountain Entrance Station, 47050 Generals Hwy, Three Rivers, CA 93271
places.parking_notes: Park at Foothills Visitor Center near Ash Mountain Entrance. Limited parking at trailheads along the highway. Road can be run as out-and-back or car shuttle to Giant Forest Museum.

activities.notes: Road run on Generals Highway from Ash Mountain Entrance (1,700 ft) up to Giant Forest Museum (~6,400 ft). Winding mountain road with vehicle traffic; run facing traffic and use caution. No dedicated lane. Spectacular canyon and sequoia scenery. Popular car-to-car shuttle run. Approximately 18 miles one-way.

places.name: Giant Forest Loop Run
places.address: Giant Forest Museum, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Shuttle or park at museum lot; $35 park entrance; no fees on trail

activities.notes: 6.2-mile loop combining meadow trails and logging road connectors. 6,500 ft elevation. Sequoias, meadows, historic sites. Singletrack and old road mix. Best April-November.

places.name: Giant Forest Main Trailhead
places.address: Giant Forest Museum, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Museum lot or shuttle; fills by 9am; $35 park entrance; seasonal shuttle

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-15K SEQUOIA FOREST] 6,500 ft trail running among world's largest trees. 5K Big Trees Trail, 8K Crescent Meadow loop, 15K Moro Rock connector. Surreal running experience—giants overhead. Mix of paved, dirt, boardwalk. Combine multiple trails for marathon training. Less crowded than Sherman Tree area. Must-stop: Parker Group sequoias.

places.name: Giant Forest Museum Exhibits & Beetle Rock
places.address: 47050 Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Large paved lot at Giant Forest Museum with accessible spaces. Free summer shuttle from Lodgepole/Wuksachi. Museum is free to enter.

activities.notes: Giant Forest Museum plus Big Trees Trail (paved, accessible 0.75-mile meadow loop). Museum has interactive exhibits on sequoia ecology. Beetle Rock — a large granite outcrop behind the museum — is an easy short walk for wide views. Perfect for families with young children.

places.name: Grant Grove Loop
places.address: Grant Grove Village, Generals Hwy, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: NPS visitor center lot; free with park entrance; large paved area

activities.notes: 1-mile paved interpretive loop through General Grant Grove. Second largest tree (General Grant). Open year-round—sometimes snow in winter. Holiday tree lighting ceremony each December.

places.name: Grant Grove Village Trail Running Hub
places.address: Grant Grove Village, Generals Hwy, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Village lots; free with park entrance; visitor center restrooms

activities.notes: [TRAIL RUNNING - 5K-20K FOREST MEADOWS] 6,500 ft forest trail network. 5K General Grant loop, 10K Bobcat Point trail, 20K connector to Redwood Mountain Grove. Sequoia forest running—unique experience. Gentle grades, soft duff trails. Good for tempo runs. Bear frequent—stay alert. Seasonal road closure possible winter.

places.name: Heather Lake Trail
places.address: Wolverton Trailhead, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Wolverton lot; $35 park entrance; bear canisters required for overnight

activities.notes: 11-mile round trip to Heather and Aster Lakes. Enters the Great Western Divide. High Sierra lakes, granite peaks. Elevation 7,200-10,400 ft. Often snow into July. Multi-day backpacking possible.

places.name: Highway 180 Kings Canyon Scenic Byway Run — Grant Grove to Cedar Grove
places.address: Grant Grove Village, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Park at Grant Grove Village visitor center parking area. Road run on Hwy 180 to Cedar Grove (~30 miles one-way). Best done as car-shuttle. Road open May–November.

activities.notes: Road run from Grant Grove (6,500 ft) descending Kings Canyon on Hwy 180 through one of the deepest canyons in North America to Cedar Grove (4,600 ft). Net descent but rolling terrain. Vehicle traffic; run facing traffic. One of the most scenic road runs in California. 30 miles one-way.

places.name: Hospital Rock Boulders (Foothills)
places.address: Hospital Rock Picnic Area, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93271
places.parking_notes: Hospital Rock Picnic Area parking off Generals Highway in the Foothills area (~6 miles from Ash Mountain Entrance). Paved parking with restrooms and water. Day use only.

activities.notes: Hospital Rock is a large granite boulder complex in the Foothills zone (~2,700 ft elevation) with documented bouldering on the main rock and surrounding granite outcrops. Area also features Monache (Yokuts) Native American pictographs. Year-round access (lower elevation). Rattlesnakes, poison oak, and heat hazards in summer. Popular with local climbers as a warm-weather alternative when higher elevations are snowbound.

places.name: Hotel Creek – Lewis Creek Loop
places.address: Cedar Grove Visitor Center, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Park at Cedar Grove Visitor Center / Village. Same trailhead as Cedar Grove Overlook. Passes through 2024 Happy Fire burn area.

activities.notes: 8-mile round-trip loop (or 1.25 miles beyond Cedar Grove Overlook) joining Lewis Creek Trail through a burn area from 1980 and the 2024 Happy Fire. Last 2 miles follows trail above roadway. Strenuous; allow ~5 hours. Excellent canyon and backcountry views.

places.name: Knapp's Cabin & River Walk (Cedar Grove)
places.address: Knapp's Cabin Turnout, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Roadside turnout/pullout 2 miles east of Cedar Grove Village on Hwy 180. Very small, a few cars. No formal lot.

activities.notes: Very short flat walk to Knapp's Cabin, a historic 1920s stone fishing cabin used by George Knapp for fishing expeditions. Easy riverside stroll suitable for all ages. Combine with river viewpoints nearby.

places.name: Lakes Trail (Four Lakes Loop)
places.address: Wolverton Trailhead, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Wolverton Parking Area off Wolverton Rd. Large paved lot. Popular trailhead; arrive early on weekends.

activities.notes: 13-mile loop from Wolverton through Heather, Aster, Emerald, and Pear Lakes (loop option via Watchtower). Alpine lake scenery above treeline. Watchtower route includes a knife-edge ridge with dramatic views. Can be done as long day hike. Snow may block trail into July. Wildflowers peak in July–August.

places.name: Little Baldy Granite Dome — Scrambling & Bouldering
places.address: Little Baldy Saddle Trailhead, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Small pullout / parking area at Little Baldy Saddle on Generals Highway between Grant Grove and Lodgepole. Limited to a few cars. No facilities.

activities.notes: NPS lists Little Baldy (just over 8,000 ft) as a park climbing area accessible via 1.5-mile trail from Little Baldy Saddle. Granite dome with bouldering on summit slabs and lower flanks. Views of Great Western Divide. Suitable for beginner to intermediate. No technical gear required for basic scrambling.

places.name: Mist Falls
places.address: Road's End Trailhead, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Road's End parking at end of Hwy 180 past Road's End Permit Station. Large gravel lot. No additional fee beyond park entry. Open May–November.

activities.notes: 8-mile round-trip moderate hike to Mist Falls, one of the largest waterfalls in Sequoia and Kings Canyon. Sandy trail follows glaciated South Fork Canyon past impressive rapids and cascades. First part relatively flat; final mile gains 600 ft. Allow 4-5 hours. Good gateway hike for Rae Lakes Loop.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow May–June from snowmelt

places.name: Mist Falls Trail
places.address: Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Canyon parking area; free; seasonal—road closes with snow

activities.notes: 9-mile round trip along South Fork Kings River to Mist Falls. Gradual climb through canyon. Falls spray visible from trail. Continue to Paradise Valley for backpacking. Best April-October.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow May–June from snowmelt

places.name: Mist Falls Trail Run (Road's End, Cedar Grove)
places.address: Road's End Trailhead, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Road's End parking area at end of Hwy 180, past Road's End Permit Station. Large gravel lot. No fee beyond park entry. Open May–November.

activities.notes: 8-mile round-trip trail run to Mist Falls, one of the largest waterfalls in Sequoia and Kings Canyon. First ~3 miles relatively flat along South Fork Kings River through forest and chaparral with impressive rapids. Final mile gains 600 ft to falls. Sandy trail; some rocky sections.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow May–June from snowmelt

places.name: Monarch Lakes (Mineral King)
places.address: Sawtooth Trail Parking, Mineral King Rd, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Sawtooth/Monarch Lakes trailhead parking area 1 mile past Mineral King Ranger Station at end of Mineral King Rd. Very narrow, steep, winding road — RVs and trailers prohibited. 25-mile drive from Hwy 198 taking ~1.5 hours. Marmots damage vehicles; use fencing/chicken wire provided or wrap wheel wells.

activities.notes: 8.4-mile round trip (4.2 mi one-way) to Monarch Lakes from Sawtooth trailhead at 7,800 ft. Traverses meadows, red fir forest, and avalanche-scoured Chihuahua Bowl with views of Timber Gap and Great Western Divide. Footing very loose on upper section. Early start recommended; west-facing slope.

places.name: Moro Rock Trail
places.address: Moro Rock Trailhead, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Moro Rock lot; shuttle access; fills early; $35 park entrance

activities.notes: Short staircase climb up Moro Rock for spectacular 360-degree views. Handrails throughout; appropriate for children who are comfortable with heights. The paved staircase hugs the dome's edge — exposure on both sides adds to the drama. One of Sequoia's classic experiences. Allow 30–45 minutes.

Staircase climb up Moro Rock for spectacular 360-degree views. Handrails throughout; appropriate for children comfortable with heights. The paved staircase hugs the dome's edge with exposure on both sides — thrilling, not dangerous. Classic Sequoia experience. Allow 30–45 minutes.
0.5-mile climb to 6,725 ft dome summit. 400 steps carved in granite. Exposed stairs with handrails. Panoramic views of Great Western Divide, San Joaquin Valley. Fear of heights caution.
activities.conditions_note: Staircase may be icy in early season; exposed summit — clear days best for views

activities.notes: 0.5-mile round trip with 300-foot gain via carved stone staircase up Moro Rock — a giant granite dome at 6,725 ft. Handrails and steps make it accessible to most visitors despite the steep grade. Panoramic views of the Great Western Divide and the San Joaquin Valley on clear days. One of the most rewarding short walks in the parks.

0.5-mile round trip with 300-foot gain via carved stone staircase up Moro Rock — a granite dome at 6,725 ft. Handrails throughout. Panoramic views of the Great Western Divide and San Joaquin Valley on clear days. One of the most rewarding short walks in the parks.

places.name: Moro Rock — West Face Rock Climbing & Bouldering
places.address: Moro Rock Trailhead, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Small Moro Rock parking area 2 miles off Generals Hwy on Moro Rock/Crescent Meadow Rd. Summer shuttle recommended. May close during peregrine falcon nesting season (seasonal closure — check NPS current conditions before visiting).

activities.notes: NPS identifies Moro Rock west face as 1,000 vertical feet of cracks and knobs — one of the easiest climbing sites in the park. Lower granite slabs around the base offer bouldering. Approach via short trail to base. Seasonal closure during peregrine falcon nesting (summer). No power drills or permanent fixed anchors without special use permit.
activities.conditions_note: Staircase may be icy in early season; exposed summit — clear days best for views

places.name: North Grove Loop
places.address: General Grant Tree Trailhead, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Same General Grant Tree Parking Area, 1 mile northwest of Kings Canyon Visitor Center. Large paved lot.

activities.notes: Lightly traveled 1.5-mile loop through meadows, creeks, mixed conifer and sequoia forest near Grant Grove. Quieter alternative to the General Grant Tree Trail. Gentle terrain suitable for most ages.

places.name: Panoramic Point (Drive-up then short walk)
places.address: Panoramic Point, Grant Grove Village, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Grant Grove access road; paved; free with park entrance; closes in heavy snow

activities.notes: Quick 1-mile walk from Grant Grove to a sweeping Kings Canyon viewpoint. Short enough for most families with young children. Trailhead starts near the RV dump station at Grant Grove Village. Views reward the modest climb. Ideal add-on after visiting the General Grant Tree.

Quick 1-mile walk from Grant Grove to a sweeping Kings Canyon viewpoint. Short enough for most families with young children. Trailhead near the RV dump station at Grant Grove Village. Views reward the modest climb. Good add-on after visiting the General Grant Tree.

0.4-mile paved path to 7,520 ft viewpoint—more accessible summit experience. 360° views of Great Western Divide, Sierra Crest. Good for beginners, children, those testing altitude. Interpretive signs. Combine with Grant Grove loop for longer outing.
activities.conditions_note: High elevation; afternoon thunderstorms possible — off summit by 1pm

activities.notes: Short 1-mile round trip from Grant Grove Village to Panoramic Point at 7,520 ft — the best viewpoint in Kings Canyon accessible on foot. Views of the Kings Canyon, Hume Lake canyon, and on clear days the San Joaquin Valley. Trail is unpaved but well-graded. Combines well with the Grant Grove area walks.

Short 1-mile round trip from Grant Grove Village to Panoramic Point at 7,520 ft — the best viewpoint in Kings Canyon accessible on foot. Views of the Kings River canyon, Hume Lake area, and on clear days the San Joaquin Valley. Unpaved but well-graded. Combines well with the Grant Grove walks.

places.name: Rae Lakes Loop
places.address: Roads End, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Roads End lot; free; wilderness permit required for overnight; limited spaces

activities.notes: 41-mile backpacking loop (multiple days) through some of Sierra's finest high country. Alpine lakes, passes over 11,000 ft, John Muir Trail section. Rae Lakes, Glen Pass. Permit required.

places.name: Roaring River Falls
places.address: Roaring River Falls Trailhead, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Designated pull-out parking area 3 miles east of Cedar Grove Village on Hwy 180. Paved, accessible parking. Improved trail paving and reduced grades since 2017.

activities.notes: Flat 0.25-mile paved walk from the roadside pullout to a powerful waterfall crashing into a granite bowl — one of the most dramatic short walks in Kings Canyon. Accessible for all ages and abilities. Paved path to viewing platform. The falls are loudest and most impressive June–July.

Flat 0.25-mile paved walk from roadside pullout to a powerful waterfall crashing into a granite bowl — one of the most dramatic short walks in Kings Canyon. Accessible for all ages. Paved path to viewing platform. Loudest and most impressive June–July.

Very short paved accessible walk to a powerful waterfall rushing through a narrow granite chute. Shaded, cool, and dramatic. One of the easiest highlights in Cedar Grove. Trail is paved and relatively accessible per NPS.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow May–June

places.name: Sequoia Half Marathon (Race Event)
places.address: Three Rivers, CA 93271 (near Sequoia National Park Ash Mountain Entrance)
places.parking_notes: Event-specific parking at designated lots in Three Rivers. Shuttle to start typically provided on race day. Check race organizer for current logistics.

activities.notes: Annual half marathon event run on Generals Highway through lower Sequoia National Park. Course climbs from Three Rivers foothills into the sequoia groves. Stunning scenery; strenuous due to elevation gain. Typically held in spring. Check Sequoia Parks Conservancy or local race directors for current event status.
activities.conditions_note: Annual May event — verify dates and NPS permit status before registering

places.name: Tokopah Domes — Lodgepole Area
places.address: Lodgepole Campground, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Park at Lodgepole Visitor Center / campground lot. Approach granite domes via Tokopah Falls trail or cross-country. Wilderness ethic required off-trail.

activities.notes: NPS identifies Tokopah Domes near Lodgepole as a climbing area. Granite domes and slabs above the Tokopah Valley offer multi-pitch trad climbing and bouldering opportunities. Access via Tokopah Falls trail then cross-country to dome bases. Elevation ~7,000 ft. No power drills; leave-no-trace principles required.

places.name: Tokopah Falls Trail
places.address: Lodgepole Campground, Generals Hwy, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Lodgepole visitor center lot; free with park entrance; market nearby

activities.notes: Most popular family trail in Sequoia National Park. 4.2-mile round trip to a 1,200-foot waterfall in a dramatic granite canyon, starting from Lodgepole. Trail is mostly flat and well-maintained. Kids love the river crossings and boulder hopping. Can turn around early — the canyon is beautiful throughout. Allow 3–4 hours.

Most popular family trail in Sequoia National Park. 4.2-mile round trip to a 1,200-foot waterfall in a dramatic granite canyon, starting from Lodgepole. Trail is mostly flat and well-maintained. Kids love the river crossings and boulder-hopping sections. Can turn around early — the canyon is beautiful throughout. Allow 3–4 hours.
3.7-mile round trip to 1,200-ft falls on Kaweah River. Trail along river through lodgepole pine. Dramatic canyon walls. Falls may be dry by late summer. Early morning best for shade.
activities.conditions_note: Peak flow May–June from snowmelt

activities.notes: 4.2-mile round trip from Lodgepole Campground to Tokopah Falls — one of California's largest waterfalls. Trail follows the Marble Fork of the Kaweah River through glacially carved canyon. Mostly gradual grade on packed trail. Outstanding granite scenery. Best June–July when falls are at full force.

4.2-mile round trip from Lodgepole Campground to Tokopah Falls — one of California's largest waterfalls. Trail follows the Marble Fork of the Kaweah River through a glacially carved canyon. Mostly gradual grade on packed trail. Outstanding granite scenery. Best June–July when falls are at full force.

places.name: Tokopah Valley Trail Run
places.address: Lodgepole Campground, Sequoia National Park, CA 93262
places.parking_notes: Lodgepole day-use parking near visitor center. Pay at kiosk. Trail begins beyond Marble Fork Bridge.

activities.notes: 1.7-mile (one-way) easy trail run along Marble Fork of Kaweah River to Tokopah Falls. Wide, well-maintained trail with minimal obstacles. Good introduction to trail running in Sequoia. Return same route for ~3.4-mile run. Best in late spring/summer.

places.name: Zumwalt Meadow Loop
places.address: Zumwalt Meadow Trailhead, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Cedar Grove area lot; free; seasonal road closure winter

activities.notes: 1.5-mile boardwalk and trail through meadow with Kings River. Scenic heart of canyon. Suspension bridge crossing. Wildflowers June-July. Best views of granite canyon walls.

places.name: Zumwalt Meadow — Family Walk
places.address: Zumwalt Meadow Trailhead, Cedar Grove, Kings Canyon National Park, CA 93633
places.parking_notes: Dedicated trailhead parking 4.5 miles east of Cedar Grove Village. Arrive early; lot fills quickly in summer.

activities.notes: Flat, 1.6-mile loop through meadow and forest alongside Kings River, framed by 2,000-ft granite canyon walls. Crosses swinging bridge. Sandy unpaved sections; can flood in spring. Excellent wildlife and wildflower viewing. One of the best easy walks in Kings Canyon.
